zh-tw.editorNote: 對「明明熬夜還在找其他理由解釋疲憊」的精準吐槽。上班族、嘴硬不認帳的人必收，傳同事群組會有人接「我也都怪水星」。
variations: 我累的原因只有一個，但我準備了十個藉口來避開它。
成年人的累，九成是自找的，剩下一成是嘴硬不承認的那九成。

en.content:
Half of adult life is spent inventing excuses for why you're so tired.
Meanwhile the real answer lies glowing right next to me every night,
and because I won't admit it, I blame the weather, the season, Mercury in retrograde.
en.editorNote: A pinpoint jab at hunting for any explanation except the obvious one. Essential for office workers and anyone too stubborn to admit it. Expect a 'I blame Mercury too' reply.
variations: There's only one reason I'm tired, but I've prepared ten excuses to avoid it.
Ninety percent of adult exhaustion is self-inflicted; the other ten percent is refusing to admit the ninety.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'omg after death there's no moment where I'll realize I died, it's been 4 hrs' bedtime-routine tweet; rewritten with a localized derailment framing

zh-tw.content:
我的睡前流程：刷牙、設鬧鐘、關燈、閉眼，
欸不對，人死掉之後，是不是根本不會有「我發現我死了」的那一刻？
等我回過神，已經凌晨四點，剛剛那兩個字徹底毀了我今晚的睡眠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「睡前腦袋突然開啟存在主義副本」的精準描繪。半夜會被一個哲學問題卡到天亮的人秒懂，適合在又被自己的腦袋害到失眠時發。
variations: 我只是想睡覺，結果腦袋丟給我一個關於宇宙終點的問題，然後就跑了。
睡前流程的最後一步永遠是：被一個我解決不了的大問題拖到天亮。

en.content:
My bedtime routine: brush teeth, set alarm, lights off, eyes closed,
wait — after you die, is there even a moment where you realize you've died?
By the time I snap out of it, it's 4am, and those few words have wrecked my entire night.
en.editorNote: A pinpoint portrait of your brain opening an existential side quest right at bedtime. For anyone derailed by one philosophical question till sunrise. Perfect for a night your own mind sabotaged you.
variations: I just wanted to sleep, but my brain handed me a question about the end of the universe and walked off.
The final step of my bedtime routine is always: get dragged till dawn by a question I can't solve.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'you don't have to win the night, just keep it company' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的時候，別跟夜晚較勁。
你贏不過它，它也沒打算贏你，
你們只是剛好都還醒著，那就一起待著吧。
手放在胸口，感覺自己還在呼吸，這樣就很好了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時越想「我一定要睡著」越睡不著。這句話把「對抗」換成「陪伴」，鬆開那股緊繃。傳給最近在床上跟自己拔河的朋友。
variations: 你不用打贏這個夜晚，陪它待著就好。
睡不著沒關係，把手放胸口，感覺自己還在，就夠了。

en.content:
When you can't sleep, don't fight the night.
You won't beat it, and it isn't trying to beat you —
you both just happen to be awake, so keep each other company.
Hand on your chest, feel yourself still breathing. That's already enough.
en.editorNote: The harder you insist on sleeping, the further it gets. This swaps fighting for keeping company, loosening the tension. Send to a friend who's been wrestling with themselves in bed.
variations: You don't have to win the night. Just keep it company.
It's okay not to sleep. Hand on your chest, feel that you're still here — that's enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'insomnia gives you time to win every argument in your head' meme; rewritten with a localized rematch/championship framing

zh-tw.content:
失眠最大的好處，就是讓我有時間，
把這輩子吵輸的每一場架，在腦袋裡重打一遍。
而且這次我每場都贏，金句還特別漂亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「深夜腦內重播舊吵架並補上完美台詞」的精準吐槽。嘴笨、事後才想到怎麼回的人秒懂，傳給也常常在床上贏麻了的朋友。
variations: 我半夜不是睡不著，是在開我個人的辯論冠軍賽，對手是三年前的某人。
白天吵輸的，深夜在腦袋裡通通贏回來，可惜沒有人知道。

en.content:
The best part of insomnia is that it gives me time
to re-fight every argument I ever lost, right here in my head.
And this time I win all of them, with killer comebacks too.
en.editorNote: A pinpoint jab at replaying old arguments at 3am and finally landing the perfect line. For anyone who only thinks of the comeback hours too late. Send to a friend who also wins big in bed.
variations: I'm not awake at 2am, I'm hosting my personal debate championship against someone from three years ago.
Everything I lost during the day, I win back in my head at night. Shame nobody's there to see it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Lynn Johnston's 'misery of partying all night without the satisfaction' line; rewritten with a localized hangover-without-the-party framing

zh-tw.content:
失眠最不公平的地方是：
你享受了通宵的所有疲憊，
卻一點通宵的快樂都沒分到。
隔天頂著黑眼圈，連個能炫耀的故事都沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「累得像狂歡一整晚，實際上只是躺著瞪天花板」的精準吐槽。適合在又一次毫無收穫地熬到天亮時發，配張頂著黑眼圈的自拍很搭。
variations: 我有宿醉的疲勞，卻沒有任何一場該負責的派對。
別人熬夜是去玩，我熬夜是純粹地醒著，這待遇也差太多。

en.content:
The most unfair thing about insomnia is this:
you get all the exhaustion of pulling an all-nighter,
with none of the fun.
Next day you've got the dark circles but not even a story to brag about.
en.editorNote: A sharp take on being wrecked like you partied all night when you just lay there staring at the ceiling. Perfect for another fruitless sunrise — pair with a tired selfie.
variations: I've got the hangover fatigue with no party to blame it on.
Other people stay up to have fun. I stay up to purely, simply be awake. The deal is not the same.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you're awake because you're in someone's dream, please stop dreaming about me' line; rewritten with a localized opt-out request framing

zh-tw.content:
聽說睡不著，是因為有人正在夢裡夢到你。
那我想正式公告：
請大家不要再夢到我了，謝謝配合，我真的需要下線休息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡不著的民間說法翻成一則「退訂公告」，俏皮又有梗。適合在凌晨翻來覆去時當限動文案，朋友會接「我也想退訂」。
variations: 如果失眠真的是因為被別人夢到，那我要申請從所有人的夢裡退場。
拜託夢到我的那位，能不能改天再夢，今晚我想專心睡覺。

en.content:
They say you can't sleep because someone out there is dreaming about you.
In that case, official announcement:
please stop dreaming about me, thanks for your cooperation, I really need to log off and rest.
en.editorNote: Turns the old folk belief into an opt-out notice — playful and quotable. Great caption for the toss-and-turn hour; expect friends to reply 'I'd like to unsubscribe too.'
variations: If insomnia really is people dreaming about me, I'd formally like to exit everyone's dreams.
To whoever's dreaming about me: could you reschedule? Tonight I'd like to focus on sleeping.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my body is a temple and my brain is a nightclub that never closes' line; rewritten with a localized 24-hour venue framing

zh-tw.content:
我的身體想當一間準時打烊的店，
但我的腦袋是隔壁那家 24 小時不關門的夜店，
燈光全開、音樂全大聲，還一直辦活動。
隔音又超差，我整晚都被吵到睡不著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「身體想睡但腦袋超嗨」的衝突包裝成兩家營業時間不同的店，畫面感十足。適合在腦袋停不下來的深夜發，夜貓朋友會狂笑。
variations: 我的身體掛上打烊牌了，我的腦袋還在裡面開趴。
想睡的是我，不想關門的是我腦袋，這兩個一直是分開營業的。

en.content:
My body wants to be a shop that closes on time,
but my brain is the 24-hour club next door —
lights blazing, music maxed out, hosting event after event.
The soundproofing is terrible, so I'm kept awake all night.
en.editorNote: Repackages the 'body wants sleep, brain is hyped' conflict as two venues with different hours. Highly visual. Drop it on a night your brain won't quit — night owls will lose it.
variations: My body hung up the 'closed' sign. My brain is still inside throwing a party.
I'm the one who wants to sleep; my brain is the one who won't close up. We've always operated on separate hours.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'nothing is louder than overthinking after midnight, so be gentle with the volume' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
半夜的思緒，永遠比白天大聲。
不是事情變嚴重了，只是夜裡太安靜，
讓每個小念頭都被放大成了巨人。
等天亮，它們會自己縮回原本的尺寸，別現在就相信它們說的話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最容易把小煩惱想成天大的事。這句話提醒人「是音量被放大，不是問題變大了」，幫人鬆開深夜的災難化思考。傳給最近半夜容易鑽牛角尖的朋友。
variations: 深夜想到的壞事，多半到了早上就沒那麼可怕了，先別急著下結論。
不是你的問題變大，是夜太安靜，把音量開到了最大。

en.content:
Thoughts at midnight are always louder than they are by day.
Nothing actually got worse — the night is just so quiet
that every small worry gets blown up into a giant.
By morning they'll shrink back to their real size. Don't believe what they say right now.
en.editorNote: Insomnia loves to inflate small worries into catastrophes. This reminds you it's the volume that went up, not the problem. Helps loosen late-night spiraling. Send to a friend who's been overthinking at 3am.
variations: The bad things you think of at night are usually less scary by morning. Don't draw conclusions yet.
Your problem didn't get bigger. The night just got quiet enough to turn the volume all the way up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep no longer cures tiredness, it just postpones it' meme; rewritten with a warranty-expired framing

zh-tw.content:
睡滿八小時，起床還是累。
我終於明白：睡覺不會消除疲憊，
它只是幫你把疲憊延期到下午三點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「睡飽了還是累」這種背叛感的精準吐槽。傳給每天睡很多卻一樣沒電的同事，會收到一片「對啦就是這樣」。
variations: 睡眠不是充電，是把沒電的時間往後挪而已。

en.content:
Slept a full eight hours and woke up tired anyway.
I finally get it: sleep doesn't cure exhaustion,
it just postpones it to 3pm.
en.editorNote: The precise takedown of the 'slept plenty, still wrecked' betrayal. Send it to the coworker who sleeps a lot and runs on empty anyway — expect a 'yep, exactly.'
variations: Sleep isn't recharging. It just reschedules the empty battery for later.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'tired: yes, able to sleep: no, make it make sense' paradox meme; rewritten as a customer-complaint dialogue

zh-tw.content:
身體：我好累，累到不行。
我：那我們去睡吧。
身體：不要。
我：……那你想幹嘛？
身體：盯著黑暗，重播你今天講錯的話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「明明很累卻睡不著」的矛盾寫成身體跟自己的鬥嘴。傳給也常常累到睡不著的朋友，會笑著回「我的身體也這樣」。
variations: 累是真的累，睡不著也是真的睡不著，這兩件事居然能同時成立。

en.content:
Body: I'm exhausted, completely wiped out.
Me: Great, let's sleep then.
Body: No.
Me: …Then what do you want?
Body: To stare into the dark and replay everything you said wrong today.
en.editorNote: Turns the 'tired but can't sleep' paradox into a bicker with your own body. Send to a friend who's also too tired to sleep — they'll reply 'mine does this too.'
variations: Genuinely exhausted and genuinely unable to sleep — somehow both true at once.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'daytime zombie, 2am visionary' meme; rewritten with a localized productivity-schedule contrast

zh-tw.content:
白天的我：連一封 email 都打不完，眼皮快闔上。
凌晨兩點的我：要不要學一門新語言、重新規劃人生、順便寫個小說？
這兩個人，從來沒在對的時間出現過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「白天廢人、半夜天才」這種錯位的最佳描繪。傳給也常常在半夜突然充滿幹勁的朋友，會引發一片苦笑。
variations: 我的靈感跟睡意總是錯開上班，一個白天罷工，一個半夜加班。

en.content:
Daytime me: can't even finish one email, eyelids closing.
2am me: should I learn a new language, rebuild my entire life plan, and maybe write a novel?
These two have never once shown up at the right time.
en.editorNote: The definitive portrait of the 'daytime zombie, 2am visionary' mismatch. Send to a friend who also gets a sudden burst of ambition after midnight.
variations: My motivation and my sleepiness work opposite shifts: one strikes by day, the other clocks in at 2am.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '3am is when my ex gets a novel-length text' tweet; rewritten with an unsent-draft / autosave framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，我打了一封超長的訊息，
內容真誠、邏輯清楚、感人到我自己都想哭。
還好我有一個守護天使——叫做「沒有按下發送」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜情緒上頭、差點傳出去的長訊息，幾乎人人有過。傳到朋友群組會引發一連串「還好我也沒傳出去」的告白。
variations: 深夜打的草稿，是給早上的我留的一個驚嚇。

en.content:
At 3am I typed out a very long message —
heartfelt, perfectly logical, moving enough to make me tear up.
Thankfully I have a guardian angel named 'never hit send.'
en.editorNote: The almost-sent late-night essay is nearly universal. Drop it in the group chat for a wave of 'glad I didn't send mine either' confessions.
variations: The draft I typed at 3am is a jump-scare I left for morning me.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'laughing at the sleeplessness is allowed' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
如果今晚又睡不著，那就笑一下吧。
不是要你假裝沒事，
只是被同一件事困住的時候，能笑出來的人，已經比剛剛輕一點了。
明天會到的，不用你硬撐它來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最難的是「對自己生氣」。這句話允許人用幽默代替自責，不勉強樂觀，只是鬆一口氣。傳給最近被睡眠折磨又對自己很嚴格的朋友。
variations: 睡不著的時候笑得出來，不是逃避，是給自己一條喘氣的縫。
對著天花板苦笑一下也好，明天自己會來，你不用守著它。

en.content:
If you can't sleep again tonight, try to laugh a little.
Not to pretend everything's fine —
just that when you're stuck on the same thing, the person who can still laugh is already a bit lighter than a moment ago.
Morning will come. You don't have to drag it here by force.
en.editorNote: The hardest part of insomnia is being angry at yourself. This gives permission to choose humor over self-blame — no forced positivity, just a small exhale. For the friend who's been wrecked by bad sleep and hard on themselves.
variations: Laughing when you can't sleep isn't avoidance — it's a crack to breathe through.
A tired smile at the ceiling is okay. Morning will arrive on its own; you don't have to guard it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'Wi-Fi doesn't sleep, neither do I, coincidence?' line; rewritten with a localized router-roommate framing

zh-tw.content:
家裡的 Wi-Fi 從來不睡，
我也從來不睡。
我們是這個房子裡，唯二二十四小時都醒著的室友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把熬夜跟一直亮著的路由器綁在一起的自嘲。適合在又一次滑到天亮的清晨發限動，網路重度使用者秒懂。
variations: 我跟 Wi-Fi 達成共識：誰都不准先睡。

en.content:
The Wi-Fi in this house never sleeps.
Neither do I.
We're the only two roommates here who stay awake twenty-four hours a day.
en.editorNote: Pairs your insomnia with the always-on router. Perfect caption for the scroll-til-sunrise morning. Heavy internet users will get it instantly.
variations: Me and the Wi-Fi have an agreement: neither of us is allowed to sleep first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'bedtime routine: brush teeth, set alarm, oh god I'm on Twitter' tweet; rewritten with a localized self-sabotage loop

zh-tw.content:
我的睡前流程：
刷牙、設鬧鐘、躺好、關燈，
然後——欸怎麼又開始滑手機了？
閉上眼睛，欸我怎麼還在滑？
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「明明準備好要睡，手卻自動拿起手機」的精準描繪。傳給也常常陷入這個迴圈的朋友，會回「這是在拍我嗎」。
variations: 睡前儀式的最後一步，永遠是「再看一下手機就好」，然後就沒有然後了。

en.content:
My bedtime routine:
brush teeth, set alarm, lie down, lights off,
and then — wait, how am I scrolling again?
Close my eyes. Wait, why am I STILL scrolling?
en.editorNote: Nails the 'all set to sleep, hand grabs the phone anyway' loop. Send to a friend stuck in the same cycle — expect a 'were you filming me?'
variations: The last step of my bedtime ritual is always 'just one more look at my phone.' There is no step after that.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'furious with each of my pillows individually' insomnia tweet; rewritten with a localized one-by-one grievance framing

zh-tw.content:
失眠之前，我不知道一個人可以對自己的枕頭，
生這麼大的氣。
而且是一顆一顆，分開生氣。
太低、太高、太熱、太硬——每顆都有罪。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡不著時遷怒寢具的荒謬感寫到極致。傳給也常常半夜跟枕頭過不去的朋友，會笑著回「我還會翻面找冷的那邊」。
variations: 凌晨三點，我的敵人是我的棉被、我的枕頭、還有我自己。

en.content:
Before insomnia, I had no idea a person could be this furious
at their own pillows.
And individually, too — one at a time.
Too low, too high, too warm, too hard — every single one is guilty.
en.editorNote: Pushes the 'blaming the bedding at 3am' absurdity to its limit. Send to a friend who also fights their pillow nightly — expect 'I flip it to find the cold side too.'
variations: At 3am my enemies are my blanket, my pillow, and myself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'at 3am I solved world hunger, at 8am I hit snooze 14 times' format; rewritten with a localized genius-vs-morning contrast

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的我：想通了人生方向、規劃好存錢計畫、決定明天要早起運動。
早上七點的我：把鬧鐘按掉第十四次，連自己叫什麼名字都想不起來。
這兩個我，從來沒見過面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「深夜超有智慧、白天完全失能」的落差最精準的吐槽。傳給也常常半夜立志、早上崩潰的朋友，共鳴爆表。
variations: 我半夜的決心，活不過第一聲鬧鐘。

en.content:
Me at 3am: figured out my life direction, mapped a savings plan, decided to wake up early and exercise.
Me at 7am: hit snooze for the fourteenth time, can't even remember my own name.
These two have never met.
en.editorNote: The sharpest take on the 'midnight genius, morning disaster' gap. Send to a friend who also makes grand 2am vows and crumbles by sunrise.
variations: My late-night resolutions don't survive the first alarm.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'you don't owe the night anything' angle to the healing register established by iq-008, iq-035, iq-115

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的時候，你不欠這個夜晚一個交代。
不用逼自己快點睡著，也不用想出睡不著的原因。
你只是醒著，這樣就好。
天亮不需要你同意，它自己會來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常陷入「我一定要趕快睡著」的二次焦慮。這句話把「責任」從夜晚拿走，讓人鬆一口氣。傳給最近被睡眠折磨又對自己很嚴格的朋友。
variations: 你不用對睡不著這件事負責，躺著，等天亮，就已經夠了。
夜晚不是考試，沒睡著也不會被扣分。

en.content:
When you can't sleep, you don't owe this night an explanation.
You don't have to force yourself under, or figure out why you're awake.
You're just awake. That's allowed.
Morning doesn't need your permission — it'll come on its own.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often spiral into 'I have to fall asleep right now' anxiety. This takes the responsibility off the night, so they can exhale. For the friend who's been wrecked by bad sleep and hard on themselves.
variations: You're not responsible for not sleeping. Lying there, waiting for morning, is already enough.
The night isn't a test. You don't lose points for staying awake.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the classic 'I wake up tired and go to bed wide awake' line; rewritten with a localized reversed-schedule framing

zh-tw.content:
我的作息是反過來裝的：
早上起床累到不行，晚上躺平精神超好。
白天像沒充電的手機，半夜像剛換了一顆新電池。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「該醒的時候睏、該睡的時候醒」這個經典悖論的最佳吐槽。傳給生理時鐘完全壞掉的朋友，會回「這就是我本人」。
variations: 我不是累，我只是把清醒的額度都用在不該用的時段。

en.content:
My schedule was installed backwards:
I wake up exhausted and lie down wide awake.
Daytime I'm a phone that never charged, midnight I'm running on a brand-new battery.
en.editorNote: The definitive comeback to the 'sleepy when I should be up, alert when I should be down' paradox. Send to a friend whose body clock is fully broken — expect a 'this is literally me.'
variations: I'm not tired. I just spend my whole alertness budget in the wrong time slots.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my bed is where I suddenly remember everything I was supposed to do' line; rewritten with a localized inbox/reminder framing

zh-tw.content:
我的床是全世界記憶力最好的地方。
一躺下去，所有我忘記回的訊息、沒繳的帳單、答應別人卻沒做的事，
全部排隊跳出來跟我打招呼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「一躺下就突然想起一堆待辦」的精準描繪。健忘又失眠的人秒懂，傳群組會有人接「然後我還是沒去做」。
variations: 白天什麼都記不住，一躺到床上記憶體全部解鎖。

en.content:
My bed is the place with the best memory on earth.
The second I lie down, every text I forgot to reply to, every unpaid bill, every favor I promised and never did
lines up to say hello.
en.editorNote: Nails the 'lie down and suddenly remember the entire to-do list' moment. Instantly relatable for the forgetful-and-sleepless crowd — expect a 'and I still don't get up to do it.'
variations: Can't remember a thing all day, but the moment I hit the mattress my memory unlocks completely.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'as soon as I go to bed my body is like JUST KIDDING' line; rewritten with a localized body-betrayal framing

zh-tw.content:
我累了一整天，終於爬上床。
結果身體說：開玩笑的啦。
然後幫我把睏意全部關掉，順便打開「思考人生」模式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「明明很睏，一躺下就完全清醒」這個身體背叛瞬間的最佳吐槽。傳給也常常被自己身體耍的朋友，會笑著回「它每天都這樣整我」。
variations: 我的身體很會挑時間，總在我躺平那一秒，宣布今天不睡了。

en.content:
Exhausted all day, I finally climb into bed.
Then my body goes: just kidding.
It switches off all the sleepiness and helpfully opens 'contemplate your entire life' mode instead.
en.editorNote: The definitive take on the body-betrayal moment — dead tired, then bolt awake the second you lie down. Send to a friend whose own body pranks them nightly.
variations: My body has great timing. The exact second I lie down, it announces we're not sleeping tonight.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'insomnia gives you more time to win every argument in your head' tweet; rewritten with a localized rematch/championship framing

zh-tw.content:
失眠唯一的好處，是讓我有時間，
把這輩子吵輸的架，全部重打一遍。
凌晨三點的我，口才一流，每一場都贏。
可惜對方都在睡，而且根本不知道我們在吵。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「半夜在腦內重演吵架還反敗為勝」的精準描繪。傳給也常常深夜召開「假想辯論大賽」的朋友，會回「我還準備了三套說詞」。
variations: 我半夜贏的每一場架，對方連自己有參加都不知道。

en.content:
The one upside of insomnia is the extra time
to re-fight every argument I ever lost.
Me at 3am is eloquent and undefeated.
Shame the other side is asleep and has no idea we're even fighting.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint portrait of the 3am mental rematch where you finally win. Send to a friend who also hosts imaginary debates at night — expect 'I've got three comebacks loaded.'
variations: Every argument I win at night, the other person doesn't even know they entered.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'you're not racing the night' angle to the healing register established by iq-008, iq-035, iq-120

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的時候，先放下「我一定要睡著」這件事。
你不是在跟夜晚比賽，也沒有人在計時。
累了就閉眼，閉著眼睛休息，也是在幫身體充電。
睡意會來，只是它今天走得慢一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常把「睡著」變成一個必須達成的任務，結果越急越睡不著。這句話拆掉那份壓力，把焦點移回「休息」。傳給最近輾轉難眠又很自責的朋友。
variations: 閉上眼睛不一定要睡著，光是讓眼睛休息，身體就知道你在替它好。
睡意不是叫不來，它只是這幾天比較容易迷路。

en.content:
When you can't sleep, set down the 'I have to fall asleep' part first.
You're not racing the night, and nobody's running a clock.
If you're tired, close your eyes — resting with your eyes shut recharges you too.
Sleep will come. It's just walking slower tonight.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs tend to turn 'falling asleep' into a task they must complete, and the urgency only makes it worse. This dismantles the pressure and shifts the focus back to rest. For the friend who's been tossing and blaming themselves.
variations: Closing your eyes doesn't have to mean sleeping. Just resting them tells your body you're on its side.
Sleep isn't refusing to come. It's just been getting lost a little easily lately.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep and I are in a long-distance relationship, it's complicated' line; rewritten with a localized read-receipt framing

zh-tw.content:
我跟睡眠是遠距離戀愛。
我每晚準時赴約，它已讀不回。
偶爾凌晨四點現身一下，待沒幾分鐘，鬧鐘就把它趕走了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠講成單向的遠距戀，特別有畫面。適合在又一次等睡眠到天亮的清晨發限動，配張空蕩蕩的天花板照很搭。
variations: 睡眠對我若有似無，我們的關係狀態永遠是「複雜」。

en.content:
Sleep and I are in a long-distance relationship.
I show up on time every night; it leaves me on read.
It drops by around 4am occasionally, stays a few minutes, then the alarm chases it off.
en.editorNote: Frames insomnia as a one-sided long-distance romance. Cinematic — perfect caption for the morning after waiting up for sleep again, paired with an empty-ceiling shot.
variations: Sleep keeps me guessing. Our relationship status is permanently 'it's complicated.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'if overthinking burned calories I'd be a fitness model by sunrise' line; rewritten with a localized marathon-medal framing

zh-tw.content:
如果想太多會消耗熱量，
我每天凌晨都在跑全馬。
睡前胡思亂想三小時，等於繞操場一百圈。
可惜唯一拿到的獎牌，是隔天的黑眼圈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「睡前過度反芻」的最佳自嘲，把腦力消耗講成運動量。傳給也常常半夜腦袋停不下來的朋友，會回「那我早該瘦了」。
variations: 我腦袋每晚跑的距離，足夠繞地球一圈，可惜不算運動。

en.content:
If overthinking burned calories,
I'd be running a full marathon every night.
Three hours of pre-sleep spiraling equals a hundred laps around the track.
The only medal I get is the dark circles the next morning.
en.editorNote: The definitive self-roast of pre-sleep rumination — recasts mental churn as exercise. Send to a friend whose brain won't quit at night; expect a 'then I should be ripped by now.'
variations: The distance my brain runs every night could circle the earth. Pity none of it counts as cardio.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my brain starts meetings at midnight, my thoughts argue loudly at 2am' joke; rewritten with a localized debate-club framing

zh-tw.content:
別人的腦袋晚上會關機，我的會開辯論社。
正方：你今天那句話超級失禮。
反方：對方根本沒在意吧。
主席（也是我）：好，那我們吵到三點再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前內心拉扯包裝成辯論比賽，特別有畫面。傳給也常常半夜一個人吵成兩派的朋友，會笑著回「我這場也還沒結束」。
variations: 我的腦袋每晚自動分裂成正反兩方，而且兩邊都很會講。

en.content:
Other people's brains power down at night; mine starts a debate club.
Pro: That thing you said today was so rude.
Con: They clearly didn't even notice.
Chair (also me): Great, let's argue about it until 3am.
en.editorNote: Repackages the pre-sleep mental tug-of-war as a formal debate. Send to a friend who also splits into two opposing sides at night; expect a 'mine's still in session.'
variations: My brain auto-splits into pro and con every night, and both sides are annoyingly persuasive.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'morning people must live on another planet' meme; rewritten with a localized different-species framing

zh-tw.content:
那些早上六點就能精神奕奕去晨跑的人，
我合理懷疑他們跟我不是同一個物種。
我六點還醒著，但那是因為我根本沒睡，不是因為我起床了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「晨型人到底怎麼辦到的」的最佳吐槽。夜貓族秒懂，傳給也覺得早起人類很神奇的朋友，會回「他們一定有開外掛」。
variations: 晨型人跟夜貓族，是兩種永遠在錯開時間活著的生物。

en.content:
People who can spring up at 6am for a cheerful jog —
I have my doubts we're even the same species.
I'm also awake at 6am, but that's because I never slept, not because I got up.
en.editorNote: The definitive take on 'how do morning people do it.' Instant nod for night owls; send to a friend who also finds early risers baffling — expect 'they're definitely cheating.'
variations: Morning people and night owls are two creatures permanently living on opposite clocks.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'the night is keeping you company, not punishing you' angle to the healing register established by iq-008, iq-035, iq-120, iq-125

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的夜，不是在懲罰你。
它只是比較安靜，安靜到你終於聽見自己。
不用急著趕走這份清醒，
累了就閉眼，沒睡著也沒關係，這個夜晚會好好陪你到天亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常把夜晚當成敵人，越對抗越緊繃。這句話把夜晚從「懲罰」改寫成「陪伴」，讓人不再跟它較勁。傳給最近睡不好又對自己很苛刻的朋友。
variations: 夜晚不是來折磨你的，它只是想安安靜靜地陪你坐一會兒。
睡不著的時候，把夜晚當朋友，而不是要打敗的對手。

en.content:
A sleepless night isn't punishing you.
It's just quieter — quiet enough that you can finally hear yourself.
Don't rush to chase the wakefulness away.
If you're tired, close your eyes. Not sleeping is okay too. This night will keep you company all the way to morning.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often treat the night as an enemy, and fighting it only tightens the knot. This rewrites the night from 'punishment' into 'company,' so you stop wrestling it. For the friend who's been sleeping badly and is hard on themselves.
variations: The night isn't here to torment you. It just wants to sit quietly beside you for a while.
When you can't sleep, treat the night as a friend, not an opponent to defeat.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'all my brilliant ideas come at 3am because the internet is closed' line; rewritten with a 'genius only opens after hours' framing

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子最聰明的點子，全都在凌晨三點冒出來。
為什麼偏偏挑這個時間？
因為這是唯一沒人能阻止我，也沒人會記得的時段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「為什麼靈感都半夜才來」的精準吐槽。適合在床上突然文思泉湧卻又懶得起來記的清晨發限動。
variations: 我的天才只在打烊後營業，營業時間：凌晨三點到我終於睡著。

en.content:
All my best ideas in life show up at exactly 3am.
Why this hour, of all hours?
Because it's the only time nobody can stop me and nobody will remember.
en.editorNote: The perfect comeback to 'why does inspiration only strike at night.' Great caption for the morning after your brain handed you genius you were too lazy to write down.
variations: My genius only operates after hours. Business hours: 3am until I finally fall asleep.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'at midnight my mind becomes overcaffeinated CEO of regrets' line; rewritten with a localized 'night-shift manager' framing

zh-tw.content:
一到半夜，我的腦袋就自動升職，
變成「後悔事業部」的夜班主管。
白天裝死，半夜超勤，專門翻舊帳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前反芻包裝成「夜班主管翻舊帳」的職場梗，社畜秒懂。傳同事群組會有人接「我這部門還在擴編」。
variations: 我的腦袋白天是工讀生，半夜變成查我帳的稽核長。

en.content:
The moment it hits midnight, my brain gets a promotion:
night-shift manager of the Regret Department.
Plays dead all day, works overtime at night, specializes in digging up old receipts.
en.editorNote: Frames pre-sleep rumination as a night-shift manager pulling up your old mistakes. Lethal for office workers — expect a 'my department is still hiring' reply.
variations: My brain's a part-timer by day and the auditor reviewing my entire life by night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I don't need eight hours of sleep, I need five minutes of unconsciousness' line; rewritten with a 'lowered expectations' negotiation framing

zh-tw.content:
我已經不奢求八小時了。
給我五分鐘的斷電就好，
要昏迷等級的那種，醒來不記得自己是誰也沒關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡眠需求一路砍到「只求昏過去」的反諷金句。長期失眠戶看了會苦笑點頭，適合在第 N 個睡不著的夜晚發。
variations: 我不要好夢，我只要五分鐘像被拔插頭那樣的黑屏。

en.content:
I've stopped asking for eight hours.
Just give me five minutes of total shutdown —
the blackout kind, where I wake up not even sure who I am.
en.editorNote: Negotiates sleep expectations all the way down to 'just let me pass out.' Chronic insomniacs will nod through a tired laugh. Perfect for the Nth sleepless night.
variations: I don't want sweet dreams. I want five minutes of getting unplugged at the wall.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'stop trying to fall asleep, just rest' angle to the healing register established by iq-008, iq-035, iq-130

zh-tw.content:
越想睡著，越睡不著，這不是你的錯。
睡眠不是用力就會來的東西，
它比較像一隻怕生的貓，你越追，牠跑越遠。
今晚先別追了，躺著就好，剩下的交給時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常陷入「我一定要睡著」的用力迴圈，結果越緊繃越清醒。這句話把睡眠比喻成怕生的貓，鼓勵停止追逐。傳給最近一直跟睡眠角力的朋友。
variations: 睡眠像怕生的貓，你不追，牠反而會自己靠過來。
今晚不用努力睡著，努力放鬆就好，其他的別管了。

en.content:
The harder you try to sleep, the more awake you get — and that's not your fault.
Sleep isn't something you can force into the room.
It's more like a shy cat: the more you chase it, the further it runs.
Stop chasing tonight. Just lie down, and leave the rest to time.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs get stuck in a 'I HAVE to sleep' loop, and the strain only keeps them awake. This reframes sleep as a shy cat and gives permission to stop chasing. For the friend who's been wrestling with sleep.
variations: Sleep is like a shy cat — stop chasing it and it'll come sit beside you on its own.
Tonight, don't try to fall asleep. Just try to relax, and let go of the rest.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I don't do drugs, I do 3am thoughts on repeat' line; rewritten with a localized loop-replay framing

zh-tw.content:
我不碰那些有的沒的，
我只是把凌晨三點的胡思亂想，
設成單曲循環，一個晚上播個五十遍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把深夜反芻講成「單曲循環」的反諷金句。適合在腦袋停不下來、又卡在同一件事的清晨發限動。
variations: 我的腦袋不放歌單，只放同一段後悔，循環到天亮。

en.content:
I don't do anything sketchy.
I just take my 3am thoughts,
put them on repeat, and play them fifty times before sunrise.
en.editorNote: Reframes late-night rumination as a single track stuck on loop. Perfect caption for the morning your brain won't move past one thing.
variations: My brain doesn't shuffle a playlist. It loops one regret until dawn.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep is like a shy ex that won't come back' line; rewritten with a localized read-receipt framing

zh-tw.content:
睡眠像那種已讀不回的對象。
我每晚準時赴約，乖乖躺好，
它連個「在忙」都不傳，就讓我等到天亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠講成被睡眠已讀不回的單戀。畫面感十足，適合在又被放鳥的清晨配張空床照發限動。
variations: 睡眠把我封鎖了，但我每晚還是去同一個地方等它。

en.content:
Sleep is like the person who leaves you on read.
Every night I show up on time, lie down nicely,
and it won't even send a 'busy' — just leaves me waiting till morning.
en.editorNote: Frames insomnia as being left on read by sleep itself. Vivid and relatable — caption for the morning after you got stood up again.
variations: Sleep blocked me, but I still show up at the same spot every night to wait for it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain offers worst-case scenarios at bedtime' meme; rewritten with a localized buffet-menu framing

zh-tw.content:
我一躺下，腦袋就端出今晚的自助餐：
沒回的訊息、明天的報告、十年前那句話。
吃到飽，免費，而且強制取餐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前焦慮包裝成「強制取餐的自助餐」，畫面感跟共鳴都很強。適合在腦袋一躺下就開始上菜的夜晚發。
variations: 腦袋的睡前菜單沒有選項，只有一句：全部都要想一遍。

en.content:
The second I lie down, my brain rolls out tonight's buffet:
an unanswered text, tomorrow's report, that thing I said ten years ago.
All-you-can-eat, free, and pickup is mandatory.
en.editorNote: Repackages bedtime anxiety as a buffet you're forced to eat from. Vivid and instantly relatable — drop it on the night your brain won't stop serving.
variations: My brain's bedtime menu has no options. Just one line: think about all of it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'exhausted all day, wired at night' observation; rewritten with a localized battery-percentage framing

zh-tw.content:
我的電量很玄：
白天開會、走路、吃飯，只有 3%。
一躺上床，瞬間充到 100%，還想出門跑馬拉松。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「白天累爆、晚上超清醒」的精準吐槽。社畜跟夜貓族都會狂點頭，傳群組會有人接「我這台也是」。
variations: 我的身體把作息設反了，白天省電模式，半夜效能全開。

en.content:
My battery is cursed:
meetings, walking, eating all day — stuck at 3%.
The second I hit the pillow it jumps to 100% and wants to go run a marathon.
en.editorNote: Nails the 'dead all day, wide awake at night' curse. Night owls and office workers will nod hard — expect a 'mine too' in the group chat.
variations: My body installed the schedule backwards: power-saving mode by day, full performance at 2am.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — extends the healing register established by iq-008, iq-035, iq-130, iq-134 with a 'tomorrow doesn't need you perfect' angle

zh-tw.content:
你今晚不用睡得很好，明天才有資格過。
就算只睡一點點，天還是會亮，
你還是可以慢慢走進新的一天。
睡不夠的你，依然值得被溫柔對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常覺得「沒睡好＝明天不配好好過」。這句話拆掉那個條件，給出無條件的許可。傳給最近一直睡不好又對自己很嚴格的朋友。
variations: 明天不會因為你今晚沒睡好就取消，它還是會來接你。
睡得少的你，不是壞掉的你，只是累了的你。

en.content:
You don't have to sleep well tonight to deserve tomorrow.
Even if you only get a little, the sun still rises,
and you can still walk slowly into a new day.
The under-slept version of you is still worth being gentle with.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often feel 'bad sleep = I don't deserve a good tomorrow.' This removes that condition and offers unconditional permission. For the friend who's been sleeping badly and is hard on themselves.
variations: Tomorrow doesn't get cancelled because you slept badly tonight. It still comes to get you.
The you who barely slept isn't a broken you — just a tired one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'thoughts at night are like a browser with 47 tabs open' format; rewritten with a 'none of which I opened' ownership twist

zh-tw.content:
我睡前的腦袋，像一個開了 47 個分頁的瀏覽器。
每個都在跑，每個都很吵，
而且沒有一個是我自己點開的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前思緒亂竄包裝成瀏覽器分頁失控，數位族秒懂。適合在被腦內噪音吵到睡不著的時候發限動。
variations: 我的腦袋分頁全部當機，但就是關不掉。
睡前的腦袋有 47 個分頁，記憶體早就爆了。

en.content:
My pre-sleep brain is a browser with 47 tabs open.
They're all running, all loud,
and not a single one was opened by me.
en.editorNote: Frames racing bedtime thoughts as an out-of-control browser. Instantly relatable to anyone online. Caption for when the mental noise won't let you sleep.
variations: All my brain tabs are frozen and none of them will close.
Pre-sleep brain: 47 tabs, memory maxed out, fan screaming.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the early bird catches the worm but the night owl catches the best memes' line; rewritten with a localized late-night feed framing

zh-tw.content:
他們說早起的鳥兒有蟲吃。
但熬夜的我，滑到了全網最好笑的迷因。
蟲我不要，迷因我全收。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夜貓族的驕傲宣言，把熬夜講得理直氣壯。適合在凌晨笑到失眠的時候，配張迷因截圖發限動。
variations: 早起的鳥有蟲吃，晚睡的我有宵夜跟好片看。
我不當早起的鳥，我當凌晨三點的迷因收藏家。

en.content:
They say the early bird catches the worm.
But staying up late, I caught the funniest meme on the entire internet.
Keep the worm. I'll take the meme.
en.editorNote: A proud night-owl manifesto that makes staying up sound like a flex. Perfect caption for the 3am scroll, paired with the meme that got you.
variations: Early bird gets the worm. Late me gets snacks and a great movie.
I'm not the early bird. I'm the 3am meme collector.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain boots up like an old computer the moment my head hits the pillow' line; rewritten with a localized slow-startup framing

zh-tw.content:
我的腦袋白天像當機的電腦，怎麼點都沒反應。
一躺上床，它立刻開機，
而且還跑了三個我沒安裝的更新。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「白天遲鈍、躺下秒清醒」的反差講成老電腦開機，特別有畫面。適合在又被腦袋強制更新吵到睡不著的清晨發。
variations: 白天我的腦袋轉圈圈讀取，半夜它直接全速運轉。
我的腦袋設定錯了：該開機時當機，該關機時開機。

en.content:
All day my brain is a crashed computer — click all you want, no response.
The second I lie down, it boots right up,
and runs three updates I never installed.
en.editorNote: Turns the 'sluggish by day, wide awake on the pillow' contrast into an old-PC startup bit. Cinematic. Caption for the morning your brain force-updated you awake.
variations: By day my brain shows the loading spinner. At 2am it runs at full speed.
My brain's settings are swapped: crashes when I need it on, boots up when I need it off.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the quieter the room gets, the louder my thoughts get' line; rewritten with a localized volume-knob framing distinct from iq-037

zh-tw.content:
房間越安靜，我的念頭就越大聲。
燈一關，全世界都靜下來，
只有我的腦袋，開始開演唱會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「安靜反而睡不著」這個矛盾的精準描繪。適合在關燈後被腦內噪音吵醒的時候，傳給同樣怕安靜的朋友。
variations: 白天那麼吵我都沒事，一安靜下來腦袋就開麥。
世界越安靜，我的念頭音量就調得越大。

en.content:
The quieter the room gets, the louder my thoughts get.
Lights off, the whole world goes still,
and that's exactly when my brain starts its concert.
en.editorNote: A precise take on the paradox where silence makes it harder to sleep. For the post-lights-out brain noise — send to a friend who also dreads the quiet.
variations: All that daytime noise was fine. One bit of silence and my brain grabs the mic.
The stiller the world gets, the higher my thoughts crank the volume.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'Are you going to sleep? / Now shut up / [brain says something]' webcomic format; rewritten as a localized dialogue with a fridge-leftovers payoff

zh-tw.content:
腦袋：欸，你要睡了嗎？
我：對，要睡了，閉嘴。
腦袋：那冰箱裡那塊蛋糕，到底是誰先放的？
我：……現在幾點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前腦袋偷襲的瞬間寫成對話，特別有畫面。適合在又被自己腦袋拉回清醒的時候發限動，朋友會接「我的也是這樣」。
variations: 腦袋每次都在我快睡著時，丟一個我根本不在乎的問題。
我：要睡了。腦袋：等等，你三年前那句話對方怎麼想？

en.content:
Brain: Hey, are you going to sleep?
Me: Yes, I'm sleeping now, shut up.
Brain: So that cake in the fridge — who actually put it there first?
Me: ...what time is it.
en.editorNote: Turns the moment your brain ambushes you at bedtime into a tiny dialogue. Perfect caption when you've just been yanked back awake — expect a 'mine does this too' reply.
variations: My brain always waits till I'm almost asleep to drop a question I don't even care about.
Me: Going to sleep. Brain: Wait, what did they think when you said that thing three years ago?

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'all my brilliant ideas come at 3am because the internet is closed' line; rewritten with a localized 'office is closed, so is everyone I'd tell' framing distinct from existing items

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子最棒的點子，全在凌晨三點冒出來。
因為那個時間，沒有人可以阻止我，
也沒有人記得提醒我，這點子其實很爛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「深夜靈感為什麼特別多」的反諷解釋。適合在半夜突然有個自以為天才的想法時發，創作者跟接案族特別有感。
variations: 凌晨三點的點子，隔天醒來只剩一句看不懂的備忘錄。
我的靈感只在沒人能驗證它的時候出現。

en.content:
All my best ideas show up at 3am.
That's when nobody's around to stop me,
and nobody's awake to remind me the idea is actually terrible.
en.editorNote: An ironic explanation for why late-night inspiration feels so strong. Great for the moment a 'genius' thought hits at 3am — creators and freelancers will relate.
variations: 3am ideas: by morning all that's left is a note I can't decipher.
My inspiration only arrives when there's no one awake to fact-check it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my bed is where I suddenly remember everything I forgot to do' meme; rewritten with a localized 'inbox of unfinished tasks' framing

zh-tw.content:
我的床有一個特異功能：
只要我一躺下，它就會自動載入「我今天忘了做的所有事」。
白天問都問不出來，晚上倒是整理得清清楚楚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「一躺下才想起一堆待辦」這件事的精準吐槽。社畜跟健忘的人都會狂點頭，傳同事群組很有共鳴。
variations: 床是我記憶力最好的地方，可惜只在不該記事的時候運作。
白天腦袋一片空白，一關燈，待辦清單自動跳出來。

en.content:
My bed has a special power:
the second I lie down, it auto-loads everything I forgot to do today.
Couldn't recall a thing all day — but at night it has the list perfectly organized.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint take on remembering all your to-dos the instant you lie down. Office workers and the forgetful alike will nod hard — relatable for the team chat.
variations: My bed is where my memory works best — unfortunately only when I shouldn't be remembering anything.
Blank all day, then I turn off the lights and the to-do list pops right up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'at 2am I'm a philosopher questioning existence' line; rewritten with a localized 'unpaid overnight philosophy course' framing distinct from the corporate-meeting items

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點的我不是失眠，是在上一堂哲學課。
課程主題：我到底是誰、我為什麼活著、我剛剛是不是又把宵夜吃完了。
學費：明天的精神。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把深夜的胡思亂想包裝成沒人選修的哲學課，反諷又好笑。適合在腦袋停不下來、開始問人生大哉問的時候發。
variations: 深夜的我會突然開始思考宇宙，然後想不起來自己原本要睡覺。
兩點的哲學課從不點名，但學費都從隔天的我身上扣。

en.content:
At 2am I'm not an insomniac — I'm taking a philosophy class.
Topics: who am I, why do I exist, and did I just finish the entire midnight snack again.
Tuition: tomorrow's energy.
en.editorNote: Repackages late-night spiraling as a philosophy course no one signed up for — ironic and funny. Perfect for when your brain won't stop and starts asking the big questions.
variations: Late at night I suddenly ponder the universe, then forget I was supposed to be sleeping.
The 2am philosophy class never takes attendance, but tuition gets deducted from tomorrow-me.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — extends the healing register with a 'you don't owe the night productivity' angle not yet covered

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的時候，你不欠這個夜晚任何東西。
不用逼自己放鬆，也不用努力睡著。
就讓眼睛閉著，讓夜慢慢過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常陷入「連放鬆都要很努力」的矛盾。這句話拆掉那份壓力。傳給最近把睡覺當成任務在拚的朋友，會讓他鬆一口氣。
variations: 你不用對這個夜晚交代什麼，閉著眼睛就夠了。
睡不著沒關係，你不必把休息也做成一份作業。

en.content:
When you can't sleep, you don't owe the night anything.
You don't have to force yourself to relax, or try hard to fall asleep.
Just let your eyes close and let the night pass slowly.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often get stuck in the bind of 'even relaxing takes effort.' This dismantles that pressure. For the friend who's been treating sleep like a task to win — they'll exhale.
variations: You don't have to answer to this night. Closing your eyes is enough.
It's okay not to sleep — you don't have to turn resting into homework too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'looked forward to bed all day, suddenly wired at 10pm' tweet by Anne Thériault; rewritten with a localized hourly-escalation structure

zh-tw.content:
整天都在期待回家躺平。
晚上十點：欸我怎麼突然超有精神。
晚上十一點：精神更好了。
凌晨一點：要不要乾脆把廚房擦一擦。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「累了一整天結果一躺下就清醒」的精準寫照。傳給那種白天昏沉、晚上突然滿血復活的朋友，會收到一串「這就是我」。
variations: 我的身體很奇怪，最該睡的時候它最不想睡。
白天電量10%，凌晨一點突然充飽到100%。

en.content:
Looked forward to collapsing into bed all day.
10pm: huh, why am I suddenly wide awake.
11pm: even more awake.
1am: should I maybe go wipe down the kitchen.
en.editorNote: A spot-on portrait of being exhausted all day, then weirdly alert the second you lie down. Send it to the friend who's groggy at noon and fully charged at 1am.
variations: My body is strange — it least wants to sleep exactly when it most needs to.
Battery at 10% all day, then 100% at 1am out of nowhere.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Aparna Nancherla's 'me: time to sleep / anxiety: time for my one-woman show' tweet; rewritten as a theater-program framing

zh-tw.content:
我：好，要睡了。
焦慮：燈光、麥克風準備好，我的單人脫口秀現在開始。
今晚主題：你這輩子搞砸過的每一件事，依時間順序排好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前焦慮包裝成一場「沒人想看的個人表演」，特別有畫面感。傳給躺下就被腦袋拉去開演唱會的朋友。
variations: 我準備睡覺，我的焦慮準備開節目，我們時間總是撞在一起。
燈一關，腦內小劇場就準時開演，而且場場爆滿。

en.content:
Me: okay, time to sleep.
My anxiety: lights up, mic on, my one-person show begins now.
Tonight's theme: every single thing you've ever messed up, in chronological order.
en.editorNote: Frames bedtime anxiety as a one-person show nobody asked for — vivid and funny. For the friend whose brain books a concert the moment they lie down.
variations: I get ready to sleep, my anxiety gets ready to perform, and we always book the same slot.
Lights off, and the brain theater opens right on schedule — sold out every night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Nicole Silverberg's 'bedtime routine: ...oh god I'm on twitter' tweet; rewritten with a localized checklist loop

zh-tw.content:
睡前流程：
刷牙、設鬧鐘、喔不我怎麼在滑手機、
吃褪黑激素、閉上眼睛、我怎麼還在滑手機？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「明明準備睡覺，手卻自己打開手機」的循環自白。傳給每晚都在這個迴圈裡的朋友，他們會邊笑邊承認。
variations: 我的睡前儀式只有一個步驟，就是滑到天亮。
閉眼三秒，睜眼又是首頁，這是什麼鬼打牆。

en.content:
Bedtime routine:
brush teeth, set alarm, oh god why am I on my phone,
take melatonin, close eyes, how am I still on my phone???
en.editorNote: The confession of the 'I'm supposed to sleep but my hand opened the phone' loop. Send it to the friend stuck in the same cycle every night — they'll laugh and admit it.
variations: My bedtime ritual has exactly one step: scroll until sunrise.
Close eyes for three seconds, open them, back on the feed — what kind of haunting is this.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'falling asleep would be easy if my brain came with a mute button' line; rewritten as a product feature-request framing

zh-tw.content:
如果大腦出廠時有附一顆靜音鍵，
我大概可以省下這十年的失眠。
結果它只附了一個音量鍵，而且永遠卡在最大聲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「腦袋停不下來」包裝成產品缺漏的功能許願。資深失眠戶秒懂，適合在又被腦內噪音吵到睡不著的時候發。
variations: 我想許願下一代大腦能加上一個睡眠模式。
別人的腦袋會自動待機，我的腦袋只有「持續運轉」這個選項。

en.content:
If brains shipped with a mute button,
I could've skipped this whole decade of insomnia.
Instead it only came with a volume button, and it's permanently stuck on max.
en.editorNote: Repackages the unstoppable brain as a missing product feature. Chronic insomniacs get it instantly. Perfect for when the mental noise won't let you sleep.
variations: I'd like to request a sleep mode for the next generation of brains.
Other people's brains go on standby. Mine only has the 'keep running' option.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — extends the healing register with a 'the night isn't a deadline' angle not yet covered

zh-tw.content:
夜晚不是一份要趕在天亮前完成的作業。
你睡不睡得著，都不會被打分數。
慢慢來，這個夜晚會自己過去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常把「天亮前一定要睡著」當成倒數截止日，反而更睡不著。這句話拆掉那個期限。傳給最近一直跟時鐘賽跑的朋友。
variations: 天亮不是終點線，你不用拚著在那之前睡著。
這個夜晚沒有交期，你可以用自己的速度走完它。

en.content:
The night isn't an assignment due before sunrise.
Whether you sleep or not, nobody's grading you.
Take your time — this night will pass on its own.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often treat 'I have to be asleep before dawn' like a countdown deadline, which only keeps them awake. This removes the deadline. For the friend who's been racing the clock.
variations: Sunrise isn't a finish line. You don't have to be asleep before you cross it.
This night has no due date. You can get through it at your own pace.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my relationship with sleep is like Wi-Fi: sometimes it connects, mostly it doesn't' line; rewritten with a localized signal-strength framing

zh-tw.content:
我跟睡眠的關係，很像 Wi-Fi。
偶爾連得上，大部分時間顯示「訊號微弱」，
而且半夜永遠在跳「連線中斷」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠講成不穩的 Wi-Fi 訊號，數位世代秒懂。適合在又一次半夜斷線睡不著的時候發限動。
variations: 睡眠對我來說是吃到飽方案，但網速永遠卡在最慢那一檔。
我跟床的連線品質，大概只有一格訊號。

en.content:
My relationship with sleep is like Wi-Fi.
It connects once in a while, mostly it just says "weak signal,"
and at night it's permanently stuck on "connection lost."
en.editorNote: Frames insomnia as a flaky Wi-Fi connection — instant for anyone who lives online. Perfect caption for another night of dropped signal at 3am.
variations: Sleep is my unlimited data plan, except the speed is always throttled to the lowest tier.
The connection between me and my bed is running on one bar, tops.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep is like a time machine to breakfast' line; rewritten with a localized broken-time-machine framing

zh-tw.content:
睡覺本來是通往早餐的時光機。
但我這台壞了，
按下去只會在凌晨三點原地空轉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡眠比喻成「直達早餐的時光機」，失眠就是機器故障，畫面感很強。適合在又卡在深夜的清晨發。
variations: 別人一閉眼就到早上，我閉眼只到凌晨三點半。
我的時光機只送我到天花板，不送我到明天。

en.content:
Sleep is supposed to be a time machine to breakfast.
But mine's broken —
press the button and it just idles in place at 3am.
en.editorNote: Treats sleep as a 'time machine straight to breakfast' and insomnia as the machine breaking down. Vivid and funny. Caption for another stalled-out night.
variations: Other people close their eyes and arrive at morning. I close mine and arrive at 3:30am.
My time machine only drops me at the ceiling, never at tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Griff Niblack's 'if a man had as many ideas during the day as he does when he has insomnia, he'd make a fortune' line; rewritten with a localized productivity-irony framing

zh-tw.content:
如果我白天的點子，有凌晨三點的一半多，
我大概早就升職加薪了。
可惜這些天才靈感，只在我最該睡的時候上班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「為什麼最棒的想法都在深夜冒出來」的精準吐槽。創作者、上班族都會有感，傳群組會有人接「我也是凌晨才開機」。
variations: 我的腦袋是夜班員工，白天打卡，深夜才真正開工。
白天一個點子都擠不出，凌晨三點靈感像開閘洪水。

en.content:
If I had half as many ideas during the day as I do at 3am,
I'd have been promoted years ago.
Unfortunately my genius only clocks in when I'm supposed to be asleep.
en.editorNote: A sharp take on why the best ideas only show up after midnight. Hits creators and office workers alike — expect a 'mine only boots up at 3am too' reply.
variations: My brain works the night shift: clocks in by day, actually starts working at 3am.
Can't squeeze out one idea all day, then it's a flash flood at 3am.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the 'bed reminds me of everything I forgot' meme; rewritten as a personal assistant who only works the night shift

zh-tw.content:
我的床是全世界最盡責的提醒小幫手。
我一躺下，它就把今天忘記做的事、
明天該擔心的事，一次全部唸給我聽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「一躺下就想起所有事」的現象擬人化成超盡責但很煩的助理。失眠+待辦清單焦慮的人必收，傳給也常常被床提醒的朋友。
variations: 枕頭比我的行事曆還準，每晚準時提醒我還沒做完的事。
我白天忘光光，床一躺下就幫我全部復習一遍。

en.content:
My bed is the most dedicated reminder assistant in the world.
The second I lie down, it reads me everything I forgot to do today
and everything I should worry about tomorrow, all at once.
en.editorNote: Personifies the 'lie down and remember everything' phenomenon as an over-eager, deeply annoying assistant. For the insomniac with a to-do-list anxiety problem.
variations: My pillow is more reliable than my calendar — it reminds me of every loose end, right on schedule.
I forget everything by day, then my bed gives me a full recap the moment I lie down.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — extends the healing register with a 'breathing is enough for now' angle not yet covered

zh-tw.content:
如果你連睡著都做不到，沒關係。
你現在唯一要做的事，只有呼吸。
吸氣，吐氣，剩下的，今晚都可以先不管。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最容易被「我連睡覺都做不好」的念頭壓垮。這句話把標準降到最低——只要還在呼吸就好。傳給最近對自己很失望的朋友。
variations: 今晚不用睡好，只要好好呼吸就夠了。
做不到入睡也沒關係，你還在呼吸，這就夠了。

en.content:
If you can't even manage to fall asleep, that's okay.
The only thing you have to do right now is breathe.
In, out — everything else can wait until tomorrow.
en.editorNote: Insomnia easily spirals into 'I can't even sleep right.' This drops the bar to the floor — breathing is enough. For the friend who's been disappointed in themselves lately.
variations: You don't have to sleep well tonight. Just breathing is enough.
Not being able to fall asleep is okay. You're still breathing, and that's enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'most impressed I've ever been is watching someone just fall asleep' tweet; rewritten as envy of a partner's superpower

zh-tw.content:
我旁邊的人躺下三分鐘就睡著了。
我看著他，覺得這是我這輩子見過最厲害的特異功能。
而我還在原地，等睡意來面試。
zh-tw.editorNote: 描述那種「身邊的人秒睡、自己卻清醒到天亮」的無力感。適合傳給另一半，或發在羨慕好眠者的深夜限動。
variations: 有人一沾枕頭就斷電，我一沾枕頭就開機。
他把睡覺當呼吸，我把睡覺當考試。

en.content:
The person next to me fell asleep three minutes after lying down.
I watched them do it and thought: that's the most impressive superpower I've ever witnessed.
Meanwhile I'm still here, waiting for sleep to show up for the interview.
en.editorNote: Captures the helpless envy of watching someone drop off instantly while you're wide awake till dawn. Great to send to a partner, or as a story caption for the good-sleeper you resent a little.
variations: Some people power down the second they hit the pillow. I boot up.
They treat sleep like breathing. I treat it like an exam.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'me: time to sleep / anxiety: time for my one-woman show' tweet; rewritten as a nightly one-person theater production

zh-tw.content:
我：好，關燈，睡覺。
我的焦慮：各位觀眾晚安，歡迎收看今晚的獨角戲。
本場演出沒有中場休息，也沒有謝幕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前焦慮擬人化成一齣不會散場的獨角戲，畫面感十足。適合在腦袋又開演的凌晨發，焦慮型失眠戶秒懂。
variations: 我要睡了，我的焦慮：燈光、音樂、開演。
別人數羊，我的腦袋在彩排一齣沒人想看的舞台劇。

en.content:
Me: Okay, lights off, time to sleep.
My anxiety: Good evening, everyone, and welcome to tonight's one-woman show.
There's no intermission, and there's no curtain call.
en.editorNote: Personifies pre-sleep anxiety as a one-person play that never ends. Vividly relatable. Post it when the show kicks off again at 1am — anxious insomniacs will get it instantly.
variations: Me: going to sleep now. My anxiety: lights, music, showtime.
Other people count sheep. My brain rehearses a play nobody asked for.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'bedtime routine spirals into existential dread' tweet; rewritten with a localized step-by-step routine that derails

zh-tw.content:
睡前流程：
刷牙、設鬧鐘、放下手機、
拿起手機、想起三年前那句話、
再放下手機、思考人死掉之後會怎樣、
再拿起手機。天亮了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「明明要睡卻越滑越清醒」的睡前流程一步步拆給你看，最後崩壞成天亮。數位失眠戶必收，傳給也常常這樣的朋友會被瘋狂認同。
variations: 睡前SOP第七步：放下手機。第八步：又拿起來。無限迴圈。
我的睡前流程只有前三步是照計畫走的。

en.content:
Bedtime routine:
brush teeth, set alarm, put phone down,
pick phone back up, remember something I said three years ago,
put phone down, contemplate what happens after we die,
pick phone back up. It's morning.
en.editorNote: Lays out the 'supposed to sleep but more awake with every scroll' routine step by step, until it collapses into sunrise. Essential for digital-age insomniacs — send it to the friend who does exactly this.
variations: Bedtime step 7: put the phone down. Step 8: pick it back up. Repeat forever.
Only the first three steps of my bedtime routine go according to plan.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — extends the healing register with a 'you're not failing, the night is just long' angle not yet covered

zh-tw.content:
睡不著不代表你哪裡壞掉了，
只是今晚的夜，比平常長了一點。
你沒有做錯什麼，慢慢等，天還是會亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最傷人的，是覺得「一定是我有問題」。這句話把責任從人身上拿開，還給那個特別長的夜晚。傳給最近一直責怪自己睡不好的朋友。
variations: 不是你不會睡，是這個夜晚今天特別長。
睡不著只是今晚的事，不是你這個人的問題。

en.content:
Not being able to sleep doesn't mean something's broken in you.
It just means tonight's night is a little longer than usual.
You didn't do anything wrong. Wait it out slowly — morning still comes.
en.editorNote: The most painful part of insomnia is believing 'something must be wrong with me.' This lifts the blame off the person and hands it back to a night that just happened to run long. For the friend who keeps blaming themselves for bad sleep.
variations: It's not that you can't sleep. It's that this particular night ran long.
Not sleeping is a thing about tonight, not a thing about you.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the 'body tired but brain wired' split; framed as two employees who never agree

zh-tw.content:
我的身體累到不行，我的腦袋卻精神百倍。
它們兩個在同一家公司上班，
但從來沒有一天想同時下班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「身體想睡但腦袋停不下來」的矛盾講成兩個永遠不同步的員工。適合在明明超累卻睡不著的深夜發限動。
variations: 身體按了下班鍵，腦袋還在開會。
我的身心兩個部門，作息完全不同步。

en.content:
My body is dead tired, but my brain is wide awake and thriving.
They both work at the same company,
yet not once have they agreed to clock out at the same time.
en.editorNote: Reframes the 'body tired, brain wired' split as two employees who never sync up. Perfect for the exhausted-but-awake hour.
variations: My body hit the clock-out button. My brain is still in a meeting.
My body and mind are two departments running on totally different schedules.

items.sourceNote: Original 3am angle — brain drafting texts it will never send; not covered by existing items

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，我在腦袋裡打了一封超長的訊息，
情緒飽滿、邏輯清楚、字字懇切。
收件人：一個我永遠不會真的傳出去的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「深夜特別想跟某個人說話但天亮就慶幸沒傳」的精準描繪。傳給也常常半夜寫小作文的朋友，會心一笑。
variations: 深夜的草稿匣，是我最誠實的地方，也是最不會被看到的地方。
三點打好的長訊息，四點刪掉，五點慶幸。

en.content:
At 3am I drafted a very long message in my head —
full of feeling, perfectly logical, every word sincere.
Recipient: someone I will never actually send it to.
en.editorNote: A precise portrait of the late-night urge to reach out, followed by the sunrise relief of not hitting send. Send it to the friend who also writes essays at 3am.
variations: My late-night drafts folder is my most honest place, and the one no one will ever read.
Typed the long message at 3, deleted it at 4, thanked myself at 5.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I sleep great, just at the wrong times' format; rewritten with a shift-schedule twist

zh-tw.content:
其實我很會睡，只是時段不太對。
該醒的時候我睡得香，該睡的時候我清醒得像剛喝了三杯咖啡。
我不是失眠，我是排錯班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把作息完全顛倒講得理直氣壯的反諷金句。日夜顛倒的夜貓、接案族、學生都會有感，適合當自介或限動。
variations: 我的睡眠品質沒問題，問題是它挑錯時間上工。
白天想睡、半夜清醒，我大概是被排到夜班的靈魂。

en.content:
Honestly, I sleep great — just at the wrong times.
When I should be awake, I'm out cold; when I should sleep, I'm as alert as if I'd downed three coffees.
I don't have insomnia, I just got put on the wrong shift.
en.editorNote: A confidently ironic take on a fully flipped sleep schedule. Lands with night owls, freelancers, and students. Great as a bio or story caption.
variations: My sleep quality is fine. It just clocks in at the wrong hour.
Sleepy by day, wide awake at 3am — I think my soul got assigned the night shift.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'you don't have to earn rest' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
休息不是你今天表現好才能領的獎賞。
就算什麼都沒完成，你還是可以躺下來。
累了就閉上眼睛，不用先跟誰報備。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常覺得「今天沒做完事，不配好好休息」。這句話把休息從「獎賞」變回「本來就可以」。傳給那個總是把自己逼太緊的朋友。
variations: 你不用先值得，才可以休息。
沒做完的事明天還在，先躺下來沒關係。

en.content:
Rest isn't a reward you only get if you performed well today.
Even if you finished nothing, you're still allowed to lie down.
When you're tired, close your eyes — you don't have to ask anyone first.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often feel they don't deserve rest on an unproductive day. This turns rest back from a 'reward' into something that was always allowed. For the friend who pushes themselves too hard.
variations: You don't have to earn it first to be allowed to rest.
The unfinished stuff will still be there tomorrow. It's okay to lie down now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'overthinking is my cardio' line; rewritten with a fitness-plan framing localized for gym culture

zh-tw.content:
別人有規律運動，我有規律胡思亂想。
每天凌晨兩點，準時開跑，
路線是「我這輩子講錯的每一句話」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把深夜反芻講成一種「規律運動」的反諷。適合在又一次躺著把往事跑完一圈的清晨發限動。
variations: 我的有氧運動就是躺著把黑歷史複習一遍，很累但瘦不了。
別人做重訓，我做重播，練的都是後悔那塊肌肉。

en.content:
Other people have a workout routine. I have an overthinking routine.
Every night at 2am, right on schedule, I set off
on a course called 'every wrong thing I've ever said.'
en.editorNote: Reframes nightly rumination as a disciplined fitness plan. Perfect caption for the morning after you've run another lap around your past.
variations: My cardio is lying still and replaying my cringe reel. Exhausting, and it burns zero calories.
Others lift weights. I lift old regrets, one rep per bad memory.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'as soon as you go to bed your body is like JUST KIDDING' line; rewritten with a prank-show framing

zh-tw.content:
沙發上：眼睛快睜不開了。
一躺到床上：欸，突然精神超好，還想到三個明天要處理的事。
我的身體在玩整人節目，主持人是它，來賓是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「一躺下就清醒」這個經典背叛時刻的精準吐槽。傳給每天在沙發上差點睡著、上床卻秒醒的朋友。
variations: 我在沙發上是昏迷，到床上是甦醒，這中間一定有鬼。
身體：假的，剛剛那個睏是騙你的。恭喜你被整到了。

en.content:
On the couch: I can barely keep my eyes open.
The second I get into bed: suddenly wide awake, and I've thought of three things to handle tomorrow.
My body is running a prank show. It's the host, I'm the guest.
en.editorNote: A pinpoint takedown of the classic betrayal — dozing on the couch, then instantly alert in bed. Send to the friend who lives this every night.
variations: Comatose on the sofa, fully resurrected in bed. Something suspicious is happening in between.
Body: gotcha, that sleepiness earlier was fake. You just got pranked.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '3am is when you have your best ideas or doomscroll' meme; rewritten with a lottery-machine framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的腦袋像一台抽獎機。
可能抽到「改變人生的靈感」，
也可能抽到「連續滑四小時別人的婚禮影片」。
今晚我又沒中頭獎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把深夜腦袋的靈感與廢廢兩極化包裝成抽獎的機率梗。適合在又一次沒抽到靈感、只抽到廢片的清晨發。
variations: 三點的腦袋是扭蛋機，投的是我的睡眠，轉出來的都是廢的。
深夜靈感跟中獎一樣，都是別人的事。

en.content:
My brain at 3am is a lottery machine.
You might draw 'a life-changing idea,'
or 'four straight hours of strangers' wedding videos.'
Tonight, once again, no jackpot.
en.editorNote: Frames the 3am genius-or-garbage swing as a game of odds. Perfect for the morning you drew doomscroll instead of inspiration.
variations: 3am brain is a gacha machine. I pay in sleep and it only spits out junk.
Late-night inspiration is like winning the lottery: always happens to someone else.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'the night isn't graded' angle to the healing register established by iq-008/014/023/027/031/035

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的今晚，不會被打分數。
沒有人在幫你算你幾點睡、睡多久、有沒有做夢。
這一夜怎麼過都行，你已經夠努力了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常把「睡得好不好」當成一種考試。這句話直接說「沒有這場考試」。傳給那個連睡覺都要求自己表現好的朋友。
variations: 沒有人在幫你的睡眠評分，你可以放過自己一晚。
今晚睡不好也不扣分，明天的你還是完整的你。

en.content:
Tonight's lack of sleep won't be graded.
No one is tracking when you fell asleep, how long, or whether you dreamed.
However this night goes is fine — you've already done enough.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often treat 'how well I slept' like a test. This says plainly: there is no test. For the friend who demands a good performance even from sleep.
variations: No one is scoring your sleep. You're allowed to let yourself off the hook for one night.
A bad night costs you no points. Tomorrow you're still the whole of you.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 三點半的天花板是我最熟的朋友，我們每天都無話可說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合失眠深夜發限動、配一張黑漆漆天花板的照片
variations: 凌晨三點半，我又跟天花板大眼瞪小眼。
全世界都睡了，只剩我跟天花板在守夜。

en.content: The 3:30 a.m. ceiling is my closest friend, and we've got absolutely nothing to say to each other.
en.editorNote: Great late-night story caption paired with a shot of a dark ceiling
variations: It's 3:30 a.m. and I'm staring the ceiling down again.
The whole world's asleep, so it's just me and the ceiling on night watch.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 我的腦袋有個很盡責的員工，專門在我躺平那一刻開始上班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合形容睡前腦內小劇場、思緒停不下來的心情
variations: 身體想睡了，腦袋卻打卡上班加班。
一躺下，腦內會議就準時開始，而且沒有散會時間。

en.content: My brain has one very dedicated employee who clocks in the second my head hits the pillow.
en.editorNote: Perfect for that racing-thoughts-at-bedtime feeling
variations: My body's ready to sleep, but my brain just started its night shift.
I lie down and the meeting in my head begins right on time, with no end in sight.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 睡不著沒關係，先別逼自己，光是願意躺在這裡休息，你已經很努力了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合安撫失眠焦慮的朋友，或給自己的溫柔提醒
variations: 睡不著的夜晚，請對自己溫柔一點。
就算沒睡著，願意閉上眼休息，也是照顧自己的一種方式。

en.content: It's okay if sleep won't come. Don't force it. Just being willing to lie here and rest means you're already doing enough.
en.editorNote: A gentle note for a friend battling insomnia anxiety, or a kind reminder to yourself
variations: On the nights you can't sleep, please be gentle with yourself.
Even if you don't fall asleep, closing your eyes to rest is still taking care of yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'brain throws a party you weren't invited to' concept; rewritten with a group-chat framing

zh-tw.content: 全身都準備睡了，只有我的大腦臨時開了一場檢討大會，還不讓我請假。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合躺平卻停不下來想事情的夜晚，發限動保證一堆人按讚。
variations: 身體已經下班，大腦還在加班，而且沒發加班費。
眼睛閉上了，腦袋卻自動切換成夜間新聞台，二十四小時不打烊。

en.content: My whole body clocked out for the night. My brain called an emergency meeting and denied my leave request.
en.editorNote: For nights when you're lying flat but your mind refuses to stop. Post it and watch the 'same' replies roll in.
variations: Body: off the clock. Brain: pulling overtime, no pay.
Eyes closed, but my head switched to the 24-hour news channel with no off button.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'if overthinking burned calories I'd be a fitness model'; rewritten with a marathon metaphor

zh-tw.content: 如果想太多會瘦，我現在應該只剩骨頭了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 自嘲深夜腦補停不下來，配一張凌晨的天花板照最搭。
variations: 要是煩惱能燃燒熱量，我早就練出腹肌了。
整晚在腦內跑馬拉松，早上起來卻連一步都沒動。

en.content: If overthinking burned calories, I'd be down to just bones by sunrise.
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating take on unstoppable late-night spiraling. Pairs perfectly with a 3am ceiling photo.
variations: If worrying counted as cardio, I'd have abs by now.
Ran a whole marathon in my head all night, and still didn't move a single step.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'bed is where I remember everything I forgot to do'; rewritten with a to-do-list framing

zh-tw.content: 床是一個神奇的地方，一躺下就會突然想起今天忘記做的每一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種明明累爆卻一躺下就開始清單暴走的夜晚。
variations: 白天想不起來的事，全都排隊等我躺下才報到。
枕頭一碰到頭，記憶體就自動更新出十件待辦事項。

en.content: My bed is a magical place where the second I lie down, I remember every single thing I forgot to do today.
en.editorNote: For the nights when you're wrecked but the to-do list boots up the moment your head hits the pillow.
variations: Everything I couldn't recall all day lines up to check in the moment I lie down.
Head touches pillow and my brain instantly loads ten pending tasks.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep is like a shy ex that won't come back'; rewritten with a read-receipt framing

zh-tw.content: 睡意就像已讀不回的對象，我越是想它，它跑得越遠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合翻來覆去越想睡越清醒的時候，感情梗特別引起共鳴。
variations: 越努力想睡，睡意就越像已讀不回，冷淡又難追。
我對床單許願三次，睡意還是決定今晚不出席。

en.content: Sleep is like a crush who leaves you on read: the harder I chase it, the further it runs.
en.editorNote: For when you toss and turn and get more awake the more you try. The dating metaphor really lands.
variations: The harder I try to sleep, the more it ghosts me: cold and impossible to win back.
Made three wishes into my pillow, and sleep still decided not to show up tonight.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'counting sheep got boring so I talked to the shepherd' format; rewritten with a sheep-going-on-strike twist

zh-tw.content: 我開始數羊，數到第四十七隻，牠們決定罷工，說我這種上班時間根本不合理。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合翻來覆去、連助眠招數都失效的夜晚。把數羊擬人化，荒謬感特別好笑。
variations: 羊數到一半集體坐下抗議，說深夜加班沒有加給。
我還在數羊，羊已經數完我今晚睡不著的機率了。

en.content: I started counting sheep. Around number forty-seven they went on strike, saying my hours were frankly unreasonable.
en.editorNote: For nights when even the classic tricks fail. Personifying the sheep makes the absurdity land.
variations: The sheep sat down mid-count to protest the lack of overtime pay for the night shift.
I'm still counting sheep, but the sheep already finished calculating my odds of sleeping tonight.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '3am thoughts are like a browser with 47 tabs I don't remember opening'; rewritten with a memory-full framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的腦袋，像開了一百個分頁的瀏覽器，
每一個都在跑，每一個我都不記得是誰打開的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合形容深夜思緒亂竄、記憶體滿載的狀態。3C 比喻讓上班族秒懂。
variations: 腦袋分頁開太多，整個人當機，就是不關機。
凌晨的我不是失眠，是背景程式太多跑不動。

en.content:
My brain at 3am is a browser with a hundred tabs open,
all of them running, none of which I remember opening.
en.editorNote: For that overloaded-RAM feeling when night thoughts spiral. The tech metaphor lands instantly with the office crowd.
variations: Too many tabs open in my head; the whole system froze but refuses to shut down.
I'm not awake because of insomnia, I'm awake because too many background apps are running.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'thanks brain for turning 3am into a TED talk on my past mistakes'; rewritten as a nightly replay format

zh-tw.content:
謝謝我的腦袋，把凌晨三點辦成一場「我這輩子做過的蠢事」回顧展，
而且門票免費，還強制入場。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合半夜突然被十年前的糗事偷襲的時候。反諷「感謝」特別有共鳴。
variations: 深夜特別節目：由我的腦袋主辦，重播我人生所有黑歷史。
別人半夜追劇，我半夜追自己的黑歷史，還一集不落。

en.content:
Thanks, brain, for turning 3am into a retrospective exhibition of every dumb thing I've ever done,
free admission, attendance mandatory.
en.editorNote: For when a cringe memory from ten years ago ambushes you at midnight. The sarcastic 'thanks' is what sells it.
variations: Tonight's late show: hosted by my brain, replaying my entire back catalog of embarrassment.
Some people binge shows at night. I binge my own regrets, not skipping a single episode.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by generic 'I'll sleep early / also me at 3:17am researching random topics' format; rewritten with an oddly specific rabbit hole

zh-tw.content:
睡前的我：今天一定要早睡。
凌晨三點十七分的我：所以馬桶到底是誰發明的？
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在明明說好早睡、卻在半夜研究冷知識的時候。時間標到分鐘特別真實。
variations: 說好十一點睡，結果三點還在查鴕鳥到底會不會飛。
早睡計畫進行到第三小時，目前正在維基百科第八頁。

en.content:
Me before bed: tonight I'm sleeping early for sure.
Me at 3:17am: so who actually invented the toilet?
en.editorNote: For when you swore you'd sleep early and ended up researching trivia at 3am. The exact timestamp makes it painfully real.
variations: Promised myself I'd sleep at eleven; at three I'm still googling whether ostriches can fly.
The sleep-early plan is now in hour three, currently on page eight of Wikipedia.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by soothing 'rest is enough / you don't have to force sleep' angle; rewritten as a gentle reassurance

zh-tw.content:
今晚睡不著沒關係，
你已經撐過一整天了，光是躺在這裡休息，
就已經是對自己很好的一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合安撫因為睡不著而更焦慮的朋友，或睡前給自己的溫柔提醒。少一點自嘲，多一點放過自己。
variations: 睡不著不是失敗，是你今天真的很努力了，先躺著喘口氣。
不用逼自己一定要睡著，閉上眼休息，身體也會謝謝你。

en.content:
It's okay if sleep won't come tonight.
You made it through the whole day, and just lying here resting
is already a kind thing to do for yourself.
en.editorNote: For comforting a friend whose anxiety spikes because they can't sleep, or as a gentle note to yourself. Less self-deprecation, more self-forgiveness.
variations: Not sleeping isn't failing; you worked hard today, so just lie down and catch your breath.
You don't have to force sleep. Close your eyes and rest, and your body will thank you too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my thoughts at night are like a browser with 47 tabs open' meme; rewritten with a localized number and phone-tab framing

zh-tw.content:
半夜的腦袋，就像開了三十幾個分頁的手機，
每一個都在跑，每一個我都不記得是什麼時候點開的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種明明很累、腦子卻停不下來的夜晚。用「分頁」比喻思緒亂飛，滑手機族一看就懂。
variations: 我的思緒有三十個分頁同時開著，偏偏找不到那個在播音樂的。
別人數羊，我在數我腦袋裡到底開了幾個沒關的視窗。

en.content:
My brain at midnight is a phone with thirty-something tabs open.
Every one is loading, and I don't remember opening a single one.
en.editorNote: For nights when you're exhausted but your head won't quit. The 'open tabs' metaphor lands instantly with anyone glued to their phone.
variations: Thirty tabs open in my head, and I still can't find the one playing music.
Other people count sheep. I count how many windows my brain forgot to close.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I'm not tired, I'm in a 72-hour disagreement with my bed' quote; rewritten as a relationship-style standoff

zh-tw.content:
我不是睡不著，
我只是跟我的床冷戰到第三天了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠講成跟床鬧脾氣，畫面很好笑。適合連續好幾天睡不好的人拿來自嘲。
variations: 我跟枕頭現在是已讀不回的關係。
我沒有失眠，我跟床只是需要一點空間。

en.content:
I'm not tired.
I'm just three days into a cold war with my bed.
en.editorNote: Framing insomnia as a lovers' quarrel with your mattress is weirdly funny. Perfect for a multi-night bad-sleep streak.
variations: Me and my pillow are on read-but-no-reply terms right now.
I don't have insomnia. My bed and I just need some space.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'why count sheep when you can count embarrassing things you've done' insomnia meme; rewritten with a specific relatable trigger

zh-tw.content:
數羊太無聊了，
我都改數自己這輩子講錯話的次數，
數到現在還沒數完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜腦內重播黑歷史的痛，很多人有共鳴。把「數羊」換成「數丟臉時刻」，苦中作樂。
variations: 我不數羊，我數那些我以為早就忘記、結果半夜自己跳出來的尷尬回憶。
羊都睡了，只剩我還在盤點今天到底哪句話說得不夠好。

en.content:
Counting sheep is too boring.
I count every cringey thing I've ever said instead,
and I'm still not done.
en.editorNote: The 3am highlight reel of past embarrassments is painfully universal. Swapping sheep for cringe memories makes the misery funny.
variations: I don't count sheep. I count the awkward memories I thought I'd deleted, now buffering at 3am.
The sheep are asleep. I'm still auditing every sentence I said wrong today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'silent nights, loud thoughts' meme; rewritten and expanded into a fuller image

zh-tw.content:
外面安靜得不得了，
偏偏我腦子裡吵得像跨年夜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 描寫夜深人靜、思緒卻很吵的反差。適合那種身邊都睡了、只剩自己清醒的時刻。
variations: 全世界都調成靜音了，只有我的腦袋忘了關喇叭。
夜越深越安靜，我的思緒卻越開越大聲。

en.content:
It's dead quiet outside,
yet my head is as loud as New Year's Eve.
en.editorNote: Captures the contrast of a silent night against a roaring mind. Ideal for when everyone around you is asleep and you're the only one awake.
variations: The whole world hit mute, and my brain forgot to turn off the speakers.
The deeper the night, the quieter it gets, but the louder my thoughts play.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'at 4am I become a philosopher questioning life and why I ate pizza at 9pm' quote; rewritten with a localized late-night snack

zh-tw.content:
到了凌晨四點，我就變成哲學家，
開始思考人生的意義，
還有我為什麼晚上十點要吃那碗泡麵。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜哲學家的自嘲，把宏大人生問題跟宵夜後悔並列，落差感很好笑。適合嘴饞又睡不著的夜晚。
variations: 凌晨的我什麼都想通了，除了我為什麼還沒睡。
四點的我：人為什麼活著？我又為什麼要吃那份鹹酥雞？

en.content:
By 4am I turn into a philosopher,
pondering the meaning of life
and why I ate instant noodles at 10pm.
en.editorNote: The late-night philosopher bit, pairing life's big questions with snack regret, lands on pure contrast. Great for hungry, sleepless nights.
variations: At 4am I've figured everything out, except why I'm still awake.
4am me: why do we exist? And why did I order that midnight fried chicken?

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my thoughts at night are like a browser with 47 tabs open' quote; rewritten with localized framing and load-bar imagery

zh-tw.content:
白天我的腦袋只開一個分頁。
一躺上床，它就自己開了三十七個，
而且沒有一個是我點開的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前思緒亂竄比喻成瀏覽器暴衝，工程師和上班族都很有感。適合躺著卻關不掉腦袋的夜晚，發限動很容易被轉。
variations: 我不是睡不著，是我的腦袋當機了還在跑背景程式。
別人數羊，我在關分頁，關到天亮還沒關完。

en.content:
During the day my brain runs one tab.
The moment I lie down, it opens thirty-seven,
and I didn't click a single one of them.
en.editorNote: Framing racing bedtime thoughts as a browser meltdown hits home for anyone who works at a screen. Perfect for nights when you're lying still but your mind won't quit.
variations: I don't have insomnia, my brain just crashed and kept running background processes.
Other people count sheep. I close tabs, and I'm still not done by sunrise.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the best part about insomnia is more time to win every argument in your head' tweet; rewritten and localized

zh-tw.content:
失眠唯一的好處，
就是有大把時間，
把這輩子每一場吵架，
在腦子裡重新贏一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜腦內小劇場的最佳寫照，把「翻舊帳」變成半夜運動。適合躺著卻越想越清醒的時候，共鳴度很高。
variations: 凌晨三點的我，嘴砲功力天下無敵，可惜對手都睡了。
睡不著沒關係，正好把上禮拜沒回嘴的那句補回來。

en.content:
The one perk of insomnia
is all the extra time
to go back and win every argument
you've ever had, in your head.
en.editorNote: The definitive portrait of the 3am brain theater, turning old grudges into a midnight sport. Great for when lying down only makes you sharper and more awake.
variations: At 3am my comebacks are unbeatable. Shame my opponents are all asleep.
Can't sleep? Perfect time to finally land the reply I choked on last week.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'furious with each of my pillows individually' tweet; rewritten with cold-side-of-the-pillow twist

zh-tw.content:
失眠之後我才發現，
原來一個人可以，
對自己的三顆枕頭，
各生一場獨立的氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 翻來覆去、遷怒寢具的荒謬感，睡不著的人一看就懂。「翻到涼的那一面」是共同記憶，適合半夜怎麼躺都不對的時候。
variations: 翻枕頭找涼的那一面，找到天亮，兩面都熱了。
睡不著的時候，連棉被的重量都變成我的敵人。

en.content:
Insomnia taught me
that one person really can
hold three separate grudges
against three separate pillows.
en.editorNote: The absurdity of tossing, turning, and blaming your bedding lands instantly with anyone who can't sleep. Flipping to the cool side is a shared ritual, so it fits nights when no position feels right.
variations: Flipping the pillow to the cool side until sunrise, when both sides are warm.
When I can't sleep, even the weight of the blanket becomes my enemy.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep and I are in a long-distance relationship, it's complicated' quote; rewritten with read-receipt framing

zh-tw.content:
我跟睡眠是遠距離戀愛，
感情狀態：一言難盡。
每晚我都傳訊息給它，
它已讀，不回。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「睡不著」寫成一段曖昧感情，帶點自嘲又不苦情。已讀不回的梗現代人都懂，適合半夜想睡卻等不到睡意的時候。
variations: 睡意像個渣，說好十一點來，凌晨三點還在路上。
我對睡眠是真心的，可惜它每晚都放我鴿子。

en.content:
Sleep and I are long-distance.
Relationship status: it's complicated.
Every night I text it,
it reads the message and never replies.
en.editorNote: Reframing sleeplessness as a situationship keeps it self-deprecating without turning bitter. The left-on-read bit is universal, so it fits nights when you're ready but sleep won't show.
variations: Sleepiness is a flake. Said it'd come at eleven, still no-show at 3am.
I'm fully committed to sleep. Shame it stands me up every single night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I'm in a 72-hour disagreement with my bed' line; rewritten as an ongoing feud with framing about tossing all night

zh-tw.content:
我沒有失眠，
我只是跟我的床，
進入第三天的冷戰。
它不讓我睡，我也不跟它道歉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡不著寫成跟床鬧脾氣的冷戰，翻來覆去卻誰也不讓誰的畫面很好笑。適合連續幾晚都睡不好、快跟寢具反目的時候。
variations: 床跟我的關係很緊張，它負責躺，我負責清醒。
我們正在冷戰，它裝睡，我真的睡不著。

en.content:
I don't have insomnia.
My bed and I are just
on day three of a cold war.
It won't let me sleep, and I refuse to apologize.
en.editorNote: Framing sleeplessness as a standoff with your mattress makes the endless tossing genuinely funny. Great after a few bad nights when you're ready to declare war on your own bedding.
variations: Things are tense between me and my bed. It lies there, I stay awake.
We're in a cold war. It plays dead, I actually can't sleep.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'why sleep when you can have an emotional breakdown in 4K at 3AM' line; rewritten with premiere/screening framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，
別人都在睡，
只有我一個人，
在腦內看自己人生的檢討大會，
還是高畫質無廣告版。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜腦內小劇場的最佳寫照，把胡思亂想升級成獨家放映會。適合躺著躺著就開始回顧人生每個決定的時候。
variations: 白天沒空崩潰，只好預約凌晨三點的專場。
我的腦子很體貼，特地選沒人打擾的半夜開檢討會。

en.content:
3am.
Everyone else is asleep.
Just me, alone,
screening the annual review of my whole life,
in HD, no ads.
en.editorNote: A perfect snapshot of the late-night brain theater, upgrading your overthinking into a private premiere. Ideal for when lying down turns into replaying every decision you've ever made.
variations: No time to melt down during the day, so I booked the 3am showing.
My brain is so considerate, it schedules the life review when nobody can interrupt.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I don't need beauty sleep, I'm going for the haunted painting look' line; rewritten with dark-circles twist

zh-tw.content:
我不需要美容覺，
我走的是「鬧鬼老照片」路線。
黑眼圈是妝，
蒼白是風格。
zh-tw.editorNote: 拿失眠後的憔悴自我調侃，把黑眼圈說成造型，苦中作樂。適合早上照鏡子被自己嚇到的時候發限動。
variations: 沒睡飽不是憔悴，是我在還原恐怖片女主角。
黑眼圈這麼濃，不化妝也有煙燻感，很省事。

en.content:
I don't need beauty sleep.
I'm going for the
'cursed portrait in a haunted mansion' look.
Dark circles are the makeup,
pale is the aesthetic.
en.editorNote: Turning post-insomnia wreckage into a whole aesthetic is peak dark humor. Perfect for the story you post after your reflection scares you in the morning mirror.
variations: This isn't exhaustion, it's me method-acting as the girl in the horror movie.
These dark circles are so heavy I get a free smoky eye. Very low maintenance.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'tired but wired' / suddenly awake at 10pm phenomenon; rewritten with body-vs-brain framing

zh-tw.content:
累了一整天，
躺上床的那一秒，
身體說「終於可以睡了」，
腦子說「派對現在才開始」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 白天累到不行、一躺下腦子卻突然開趴的矛盾，睡不著的人最懂。適合明明累爆卻毫無睡意、超級無奈的時候。
variations: 身體關機了，腦子還在加班，這公司管理有問題。
越想睡越清醒，我的身體跟腦子從來不同步。

en.content:
Exhausted all day.
The second I hit the bed,
my body says 'finally, time to sleep,'
and my brain says 'the party's just getting started.'
en.editorNote: The tired-but-wired paradox, where lying down flips your brain on, is instantly relatable. Perfect for those maddening nights when you're wrecked but wide awake.
variations: My body clocked out but my brain is pulling overtime. Terrible management at this company.
The more I try to sleep, the more awake I get. My body and brain are never on the same page.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the '4pm coffee compiling a list of everyone who might be mad at you' tweet; rewritten with brain-as-overzealous-assistant framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點，
我的腦子突然很勤勞，
開始幫我列出，
這輩子可能得罪過的每一個人。
謝謝，但我沒有要看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜腦子不睡覺、硬要翻舊帳的荒謬感，社恐或想太多的人一秒共鳴。適合躺著躺著開始回想尷尬往事的時候。
variations: 腦子半夜自動播放我十年前講錯的每一句話，非常敬業。
沒人請它，我的腦子就自己加班整理黑歷史了。

en.content:
2am.
My brain suddenly gets very productive
and starts compiling a list of
everyone I might have offended in my entire life.
Thanks, but I didn't ask for this.
en.editorNote: That absurd moment when your brain refuses to sleep and instead audits old grudges hits home for anyone anxious or overthinking. Perfect for when lying down summons every cringe memory you own.
variations: My brain autoplays every wrong thing I said ten years ago. Very dedicated employee.
Nobody asked, but my brain decided to work overtime organizing my cringe archive.

items.sourceNote: Riff on the 'run on coffee and willpower' meme theme; reframed as a fake sponsorship line

zh-tw.content:
本人今日的清醒，
由咖啡因、意志力，
以及三點半那個「再滑一下就睡」的謊言，
共同贊助播出。
zh-tw.editorNote: 隔天頂著黑眼圈上班上課、全靠咖啡續命時的最佳自嘲。發群組會收到一排「同款贊助商」的哀號。
variations: 今天的我，是咖啡因跟不甘心一起撐起來的。
睡眠不足，本節目由我的胡思亂想友情贊助。

en.content:
Today's version of me being awake
is brought to you by caffeine, willpower,
and that 3:30am lie called
'just one more scroll and I'll sleep.'
en.editorNote: Peak self-roast for showing up to work or class running on coffee and dark circles. Drop it in the group chat and watch everyone claim the same sponsors.
variations: Today's me is held together by caffeine and pure spite.
Sleep deprivation: proudly sponsored by my own overthinking.

items.sourceNote: Reworked the '47 browser tabs' overthinking line into a first-person confession

zh-tw.content:
我的腦子半夜大概開了四十七個分頁，
每一個都在跑，
每一個都很吵，
而且沒有一個是我自己點開的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合躺在床上思緒停不下來、腦袋像當機瀏覽器的夜晚。想太多星人一秒認領。
variations: 腦袋分頁開太多，記憶體不足，但就是關不掉。
沒人點，我的腦子自己開一堆視窗還全部同時播放。

en.content:
At night my brain has about forty-seven tabs open,
every one of them running,
every one of them loud,
and not a single one did I actually open.
en.editorNote: For the nights your thoughts won't quit and your head feels like a frozen browser. Every overthinker will instantly claim it.
variations: Too many mental tabs open, out of RAM, still can't close a single one.
Nobody clicked anything, yet my brain opened every window and hit play on all of them.

items.sourceNote: Based on the 'fixing my sleep schedule' meme trend; reframed as an endless negotiation

zh-tw.content:
我在調整作息，
目前進度是：
每天都比昨天更晚睡一點點。
照這個速度，
下週我就能追上早班的自己了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每次下定決心要早睡、結果越睡越晚時的黑色幽默。作息崩壞的夜貓子專屬。
variations: 我的作息不是壞掉，是在繞地球一圈重新對時。
