調整睡眠中，預計繞完二十四小時就會準時上床。

en.content:
I'm fixing my sleep schedule.
Current progress:
every day I go to bed a little later than the day before.
At this rate,
by next week I'll have lapped the morning version of me.
en.editorNote: Dark humor for everyone who vows to sleep early and somehow drifts later every night. Made for night owls whose schedule has fully collapsed.
variations: My sleep schedule isn't broken, it's just circling the globe to reset the clock.
Fixing my sleep, estimated to be on time once I finish looping past 24 hours.

items.sourceNote: Reworked the classic 'bed reminds me of everything I forgot' quote into a sarcastic thank-you

zh-tw.content:
我的床是一個神奇的地方，
一躺下去，
就會突然想起，
這輩子所有還沒做完、
以及根本沒必要現在想的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 躺下秒清醒、腦內待辦清單自動彈出時的完美吐槽。壓力大、事情多的人最有感。
variations: 白天怎麼都想不起來，一躺上床全部自己排隊報到。
床：躺下的瞬間，替你複習今天的焦慮與明天的煩惱。

en.content:
My bed is a magical place.
The second I lie down,
I suddenly remember
every unfinished thing in my life,
plus a few there's no reason to think about right now.
en.editorNote: The perfect jab for when lying down wakes you up and your mental to-do list pops open on its own. Hits hardest for the stressed and overloaded.
variations: Couldn't recall it all day, but the moment I lie down everything lines up to report in.
Bed: the place that reviews today's anxieties and tomorrow's worries the instant you lie down.

items.sourceNote: Reframed the 'committed relationship with the void at 3am' line as a toxic-relationship bit

zh-tw.content:
我沒有失眠，
我只是跟凌晨三點的天花板，
維持著一段又長又穩定，
而且完全沒有結果的關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜睜眼跟天花板對看、怎麼樣都睡不著時的無奈自嘲。很適合當限動配上黑漆漆的天花板照。
variations: 我跟半夜三點是老朋友了，它每天準時，我也從沒缺席。
別說我孤單，我可是跟天花板長期交往中。

en.content:
I don't have insomnia.
I'm just in a long, stable,
completely going-nowhere relationship
with my ceiling at 3am.
en.editorNote: Resigned self-roast for those nights you're wide awake making eye contact with the ceiling. Perfect story caption over a photo of your pitch-black ceiling.
variations: 3am and I go way back, it shows up on time and so do I.
Don't call me lonely, I'm in a committed long-term thing with my ceiling.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I'm just in a 72-hour disagreement with my bed'; reframed as a full-blown feud with the bed

zh-tw.content:
我不是失眠，
我只是跟我的床，
進入了第三天的冷戰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 翻來覆去怎麼躺都不對、跟床過不去的夜晚。發限動配一張皺成一團的棉被照，超有畫面。
variations: 床：我很舒服。我的腦：現在幾點了不重要，重要的是我睡不著。
我跟我的枕頭最近關係緊張，它每晚都讓我失望。

en.content:
I don't have insomnia.
I'm just on day three
of a cold war with my bed.
en.editorNote: For the nights you toss and turn and nothing feels right. Post it over a photo of your crumpled-up blanket for maximum drama.
variations: My bed: 10 out of 10 comfy. My brain: sorry, what time is it, doesn't matter, we're not sleeping.
Me and my pillow are going through something. It lets me down every single night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my thoughts at night are like a browser with 47 tabs open'; rewritten with the crashing-browser angle

zh-tw.content:
半夜我的腦子，
就像開了四十個分頁的瀏覽器，
一個都關不掉，
而且我根本不記得是誰打開的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨思緒亂飛、一件小事想到另一件停不下來的時候。腦袋卡頓當機的比喻，滑手機的人特別懂。
variations: 我的腦子沒有睡眠模式，只有無限分頁模式。
別人數羊，我在數我腦子裡還有幾個沒關掉的視窗。

en.content:
At night my brain is a browser
with forty tabs open,
none of them will close,
and I have no memory of opening a single one.
en.editorNote: For when your mind bounces from one random worry to the next and refuses to quit. The laggy-browser metaphor lands hard for chronic scrollers.
variations: My brain doesn't have a sleep mode, just infinite-tab mode.
Some people count sheep. I count how many windows my brain still won't close.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I should get a loyalty card from the moon'; reframed as earning frequent-flyer status with the night

zh-tw.content:
我熬夜熬到，
覺得月亮應該發我一張會員卡，
畢竟我每天都準時報到，
從不缺席。
zh-tw.editorNote: 連續好幾晚睡不著、把凌晨當固定行程時的自嘲。適合當熬夜限動的收尾金句。
variations: 凌晨三點是我的常客時段，可惜沒有集點。
如果失眠可以累積里程，我應該早就升等鑽石卡了。

en.content:
I've stayed up so many nights
the moon owes me a loyalty card.
I show up on time every single night
and I never miss a visit.
en.editorNote: The self-roast for when sleepless nights have become a standing appointment. Great closer for a late-night story.
variations: 3am is my usual slot, shame there are no loyalty points.
If insomnia earned miles, I'd have hit diamond status by now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the moment my head hits the pillow my brain boots up like an old computer'; rewritten with the slow-startup gag

zh-tw.content:
我白天到哪都能睡，
但頭一碰到枕頭，
我的腦子就像老舊電腦，
慢慢開機，然後開始跑更新。
zh-tw.editorNote: 白天狂點頭、晚上一躺下反而清醒的矛盾夜晚。開機跑更新的比喻很好笑又很真實。
variations: 白天：關機狀態。晚上：正在安裝三十七項更新，請勿關閉。
我的腦子選在最不該開機的時候，決定重新啟動。

en.content:
I can fall asleep anywhere during the day,
but the second my head hits the pillow
my brain boots up like an old computer
and starts installing updates.
en.editorNote: For the cruel irony of nodding off all day, then snapping wide awake the moment you lie down. The slow-startup gag is funny because it's true.
variations: Daytime: powered off. Nighttime: installing 37 updates, do not turn off.
My brain picks the worst possible moment to reboot.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'replay that awkward handshake from 2016' and 'if overthinking burned calories'; combined into a late-night replay bit

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，
我的腦子突然想起，
我十年前那句講錯的話，
然後決定，我們今晚就來好好複習。
zh-tw.editorNote: 半夜莫名其妙翻出陳年黑歷史、越想越睡不著的時候。這種深夜自我羞辱大會，大家都參加過。
variations: 如果想東想西會消耗熱量，我這幾個晚上早就瘦成一道閃電了。
白天想不起來的事，一躺下全部自動上線報到。

en.content:
3am, and my brain suddenly remembers
that dumb thing I said ten years ago,
then decides tonight is the perfect night
to go over it in full detail.
en.editorNote: For when your mind digs up ancient cringe and won't let it go. Everyone's been to this midnight self-humiliation ceremony.
variations: If overthinking burned calories, I'd be a supermodel by sunrise.
Couldn't recall a thing all day, but the moment I lie down every old memory clocks in.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I don't count sheep, I count how many hours until I wake up'; rewritten as a shrinking-math countdown spiral

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點半，我開始算數學：
現在睡著的話，還能睡四個半小時。
算完抬頭，已經兩點五十了，只剩四小時十分。
我不是在睡覺，我是在倒數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種越算「還能睡幾小時」、越算越清醒的夜晚。這種心算折磨，熬夜的人一看就懂。
variations: 我不數綿羊，我數的是離鬧鐘響還剩幾個小時。
每算一次剩幾小時，那個數字就自動幫我扣掉十分鐘。

en.content:
2:30am, and I start doing math:
if I fall asleep now, I still get four and a half hours.
I look up — it's 2:50, so now it's four hours ten.
I'm not sleeping, I'm running a countdown.
en.editorNote: For the night where calculating 'how many hours left' only makes you more awake. Every insomniac has run this exact math.
variations: I don't count sheep. I count how many hours are left until the alarm.
Every time I recheck the hours left, the number quietly docks itself another ten minutes.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'My bed is a magical place where I suddenly remember everything I was supposed to do'; rewritten with a punchline about it being useless at 3am

zh-tw.content:
我的床是個神奇的地方。
只要一躺上去，白天忘得一乾二淨的待辦事項，
就會全部自動跳出來排隊提醒我。
偏偏現在是凌晨三點，一件都辦不了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合躺下才想起一堆該做沒做的事的時候。那種「想起來也沒用」的無力感，最好笑也最真實。
variations:
白天的我：一片空白。
躺下的我：突然變成全世界最盡責的行事曆。
我的床不是拿來睡的，是拿來提醒我今天又漏做了什麼。

en.content:
My bed is a magical place.
The second I lie down, every task I completely forgot all day
lines up and politely reminds me, one by one.
Too bad it's 3am and I can't do a single one of them.
en.editorNote: For when lying down flips on your memory for every undone chore. The 'remembering it now helps nothing' helplessness is the funniest part.
variations:
Daytime me: total blank.
Horizontal me: suddenly the most diligent calendar on earth.
My bed isn't for sleeping. It's for reminding me what I forgot to do today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my brain starts a podcast with zero replay value'; rewritten with no-ads/can't-skip/can't-unsubscribe framing

zh-tw.content:
每次我一閉上眼，
我的腦子就開始播一集 Podcast。
沒有廣告、不能快轉、也不能取消訂閱，
主題永遠是：我這輩子搞砸過的每一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合腦袋自動開台、播放黑歷史精選輯的夜晚。用 Podcast 來比喻，年輕族群特別有共鳴。
variations: 我腦內這個節目，收聽率一人，準時凌晨開播，永不停播。
別人睡前聽白噪音助眠，我睡前聽我的腦子逐條念我的缺點。

en.content:
Every time I close my eyes,
my brain drops a fresh podcast episode.
No ads, no skipping, no way to unsubscribe,
and the topic is always every single thing I've ever messed up.
en.editorNote: For nights when your mind auto-plays a greatest-hits reel of your failures. The podcast framing lands especially well with younger readers.
variations: This show in my head: audience of one, airs on time every night, never goes on hiatus.
Some people fall asleep to white noise. I fall asleep to my brain reading out my flaws, item by item.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my brain has unlimited nighttime data' and 'I'd get more sleep if my brain had an off switch'; combined into a power-saving vs unlimited-plan bit

zh-tw.content:
白天我的腦子開省電模式，什麼都想不起來。
一到半夜，它立刻切換成吃到飽方案，
開始無限暢想，越想越精神。
我只想知道，那個關機鍵到底藏在哪。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合白天當機、半夜卻思緒狂飆的人。省電模式 vs 吃到飽的對比，戳中每個想找腦袋開關的人。
variations: 我的腦子有夜間吃到飽，流量無上限，可惜只拿來想些沒用的。
如果腦袋有關機鍵，我願意付費升級。

en.content:
By day my brain runs on power-saving mode and remembers nothing.
Come midnight it switches to the unlimited plan,
streaming thoughts non-stop, wider awake by the minute.
I just want to know where they hid the off switch.
en.editorNote: For people who glitch out all day but race with thoughts at night. The power-saving-vs-unlimited-plan contrast hits anyone hunting for their brain's off switch.
variations: My brain has an unlimited nighttime data plan, no cap — shame it only streams useless stuff.
If my brain came with an off switch, I'd happily pay to upgrade.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the 3am love language is overthinking texts from 2018'; rewritten as rereading an old conversation and adding the replies you never sent

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的浪漫，
是把 2018 年那段對話從頭再讀一次，
然後在腦裡補上當時該回、卻沒回的每一句。
八年後的我，總算贏了這場對話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合半夜手滑翻到舊訊息、越滑越睡不著的時候。那種「事後才想到怎麼回」的懊惱，大家都有。
variations: 半夜的我，專門負責幫幾年前的我，把沒說出口的話一次補齊。
別人睡前放空，我睡前重播那段早就該封存的對話。

en.content:
The 3am love language
is rereading that whole conversation from 2018,
then mentally adding every reply I should have sent but didn't.
Eight years later, I finally win the exchange.
en.editorNote: For when you thumb into an old chat at midnight and can't stop scrolling. The 'thought of the perfect reply too late' regret is universal.
variations: Midnight me exists to finish the sentences that years-ago me left unsaid.
Some people clear their minds before bed. I replay a conversation that should've been archived long ago.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I'm just in a 72-hour disagreement with my bed'; rewritten as an ongoing cold-war standoff where neither side backs down

zh-tw.content:
我沒有失眠。
我只是跟我的床，正在進行一場長達七十二小時的冷戰。
它要我睡，我偏不睡，我們僵在這，誰都不讓誰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合連續好幾晚睡不好、乾脆把責任推給床的時候。把失眠寫成跟床的關係角力，比直接喊累更有戲。
variations: 我跟我的枕頭，已經三天沒講話了。
床：躺下啊。我：不要。這就是我們的相處模式。

en.content:
I don't have insomnia.
I'm just in a seventy-two-hour standoff with my bed.
It wants me to sleep, I refuse, and neither of us is backing down.
en.editorNote: For a run of bad nights when you'd rather blame the furniture. Framing insomnia as a feud with your bed plays better than just saying you're tired.
variations: My pillow and I haven't spoken in three days.
Bed: lie down. Me: no. That's the whole relationship.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I don't need an alarm clock; my racing thoughts wake me up just fine'; rewritten with a scheduled brain broadcast reading out old regrets

zh-tw.content:
我不需要鬧鐘。
每天凌晨，我的腦子會準時把我叫醒，
然後開始播報今天沒做完的、明天做不完的，
還有三年前那件蠢到不行的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合明明累得要命、卻被腦內廣播硬生生吵醒的夜晚。「腦子當鬧鐘」這設定，睡不好的人一看就懂。
variations: 我的思緒比鬧鐘還準時，還不給我按貪睡。
別人靠鬧鐘起床，我靠焦慮起床，全年無休。

en.content:
I don't need an alarm clock.
Every night my brain wakes me up right on schedule,
then reads out today's unfinished list, tomorrow's impossible one,
and that unbelievably dumb thing from three years ago.
en.editorNote: For nights when you're exhausted but your brain's broadcast won't let you rest. Anyone who sleeps badly instantly gets the 'thoughts as alarm clock' bit.
variations: My thoughts are more punctual than any alarm, and there's no snooze button.
Some people wake up to an alarm. I wake up to anxiety, no days off.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'If overthinking burned calories, I'd be a supermodel by now'; rewritten with the twist that it only burns sleep

zh-tw.content:
如果胡思亂想會消耗熱量，
那我每天凌晨躺著想東想西，
早就瘦成一道閃電了。
可惜它只燒掉我的睡眠，別的一點都沒燒到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合失眠又想減肥的雙重折磨。把「動腦不動身」的無奈寫成減肥笑話，特別戳中夜貓子。
variations: 我的腦子整晚都在做運動，只有身體躺著一動也不動。
熬夜想太多如果算有氧，我早就是健身教練了。

en.content:
If overthinking burned calories,
all my 3am lying-there-spiraling
would've made me lightning-bolt skinny by now.
Shame it only torches my sleep and nothing else.
en.editorNote: For the double punishment of insomnia plus wanting to lose weight. Turning 'brain works, body doesn't' into a diet joke lands hard with night owls.
variations: My brain does cardio all night while my body just lies there.
If overthinking counted as exercise, I'd be a personal trainer by now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'your thoughts decided to throw a party and didn't invite you'; rewritten so the host is the one person left out

zh-tw.content:
我躺下準備睡，
結果我的思緒決定開一場通宵派對，
吵得整晚沒停——偏偏還忘了發邀請函給我。
這場趴的主人，竟然是唯一被排擠的那個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合腦子嗨、人卻累癱的深夜。「思緒開趴卻不揪本人」的畫面，把失眠的荒謬感寫得剛剛好。
variations: 我的腦袋在辦跨夜狂歡，只有我這個房東睡不著。
思緒整晚熱鬧滾滾，唯獨當事人想散場。

en.content:
I lie down to sleep,
but my thoughts decide to throw an all-night party,
loud till dawn — and somehow forgot to send me an invite.
The host is the only one left out.
en.editorNote: For late nights when your brain's buzzing but you're wiped out. The 'thoughts party without inviting you' image captures the absurdity of insomnia just right.
variations: My brain's hosting an all-nighter and I'm the only landlord who can't sleep.
My thoughts party all night; the one person who wants it to end is me.
